text.NRSV: After the death of Joshua, the Israelites inquired of the Lord, “Who shall go up first for us against the Canaanites, to fight against them?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after the death of Joshua, that the sons of Israel asked of Yahweh, saying,—Who shall go up for us against the Canaanites, first, to make war upon them?
text.KJV: Now after the death of Joshua it came to pass, that the children of Israel asked the Lord, saying, Who shall go up for us against the Canaanites first, to fight against them?
text.NABRE: After the death of Joshua the Israelites consulted the Lord, asking, “Who shall be first among us to attack the Canaanites and to do battle with them?”
text.DRB: After the death of Josue the children of Israel consulted our Lord, saying: Who shall go up before us against the Chananite, & shall be captain of the war?

text.NRSV: The Lord said, “Judah shall go up. I hereby give the land into his hand.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said—Judah, shall go,—lo! I have delivered the land into his power.
text.KJV: And the Lord said, Judah shall go up: behold, I have delivered the land into his hand.
text.NABRE: The Lord answered: Judah shall attack: I have delivered the land into his power.
text.DRB: And our Lord said: Judas shall go up: behold I have delivered the Land into his hands.

text.NRSV: Judah said to his brother Simeon, “Come up with me into the territory allotted to me, that we may fight against the Canaanites; then I too will go with you into the territory allotted to you.” So Simeon went with him.
text.Rotherham: Then said Judah, unto Simeon his brother—Come up with me into the territory allotted me, and let us make war on the Canaanites, then will, I also, go with thee, into thy territory. So Simeon went with him.
text.KJV: And Judah said unto Simeon his brother, Come up with me into my lot, that we may fight against the Canaanites; and I likewise will go with thee into thy lot. So Simeon went with him.
text.NABRE: Judah then said to his brother Simeon, “Come up with me into the territory allotted to me, and let us do battle with the Canaanites. I will likewise go with you into the territory allotted to you.” So Simeon went with him.
text.DRB: And Judas said to Simeon his brother: Come up with me into my lot, and fight against the Chananite, that I also may go forward with thee into thy lot. And Simeon went with him.

text.NRSV: Then Judah went up and the Lord gave the Canaanites and the Perizzites into their hand; and they defeated ten thousand of them at Bezek.
text.Rotherham: And Judah went up, and Yahweh delivered the Canaanites and the Perizzites into their hand,—and they smote them in Bezek, ten thousand men.
text.KJV: And Judah went up; and the Lord delivered the Canaanites and the Perizzites into their hand: and they slew of them in Bezek ten thousand men.
text.NABRE: When Judah attacked, the Lord delivered the Canaanites and Perizzites into their power, and they struck down ten thousand of them in Bezek.
text.DRB: And Judas went up and our Lord delivered the Chananite, and the Pherezite into their hands: and they struck in Bezec ten thousand men.

text.NRSV: They came upon Adoni-bezek at Bezek, and fought against him, and defeated the Canaanites and the Perizzites.
text.Rotherham: And they found Adoni-bezek in Bezek, and fought with him,—and smote the Canaanites and the Perizzites.
text.KJV: And they found Adoni–bezek in Bezek: and they fought against him, and they slew the Canaanites and the Perizzites.
text.NABRE: They came upon Adonibezek in Bezek and fought against him. When they struck down the Canaanites and Perizzites,
text.DRB: And they found Adonibezec in Bezec, and fought against him, and struck the Chananite and the Pherezite.

text.NRSV: Adoni-bezek fled; but they pursued him, and caught him, and cut off his thumbs and big toes.
text.Rotherham: But Adoni-bezek fled, and they pursued him,—and took him, and cut off his thumbs, and his great toes.
text.KJV: But Adoni–bezek fled; and they pursued after him, and caught him, and cut off his thumbs and his great toes.
text.NABRE: Adonibezek fled. They pursued him, and when they caught him, they cut off his thumbs and big toes.
text.DRB: And Adonibezec fled: whom pursuing they took, cutting off the extreme parts of his hands and feet.

text.NRSV: Adoni-bezek said, “Seventy kings with their thumbs and big toes cut off used to pick up scraps under my table; as I have done, so God has paid me back.” They brought him to Jerusalem, and he died there.
text.Rotherham: Then said Adoni-bezek—Seventy kings, with their thumbs and great toes cut off, have been picking up crumbs under my table, as I have done, so, hath God requited me. And they brought him into Jerusalem, and he died there.
text.KJV: And Adoni–bezek said, Threescore and ten kings, having their thumbs and their great toes cut off, gathered their meat under my table: as I have done, so God hath requited me. And they brought him to Jerusalem, and there he died.
text.NABRE: “Seventy kings,” said Adonibezek, “used to pick up scraps under my table with their thumbs and big toes cut off. As I have done, so has God repaid me.” He was brought to Jerusalem, and he died there.
text.DRB: And Adonibezec said: Seventy kings having the extreme parts of their hands and feet cut off, gathered up the relics of meats under my table: as I have done, so hath God repayed me. And they brought him into Jerusalem, and there he died.

text.NRSV: Then the people of Judah fought against Jerusalem and took it. They put it to the sword and set the city on fire.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Judah made war upon Jerusalem, and captured it, and smote it with the edge of the sword,—and, the city, they set on fire.
text.KJV: Now the children of Judah had fought against Jerusalem, and had taken it, and smitten it with the edge of the sword, and set the city on fire.
text.NABRE: The Judahites fought against Jerusalem, captured it, and put it to the sword, setting the city itself on fire.
text.DRB: Therefore the children of Judas assaulting Jerusalem, took it, and struck it in the edge of the sword, setting the whole city on fire.

text.NRSV: Afterward the people of Judah went down to fight against the Canaanites who lived in the hill country, in the Negeb, and in the lowland.
text.Rotherham: And, afterwards, the sons of Judah went down to make war upon the Canaanites,—dwelling in the hill country, and in the south, and in the lowland.
text.KJV: And afterward the children of Judah went down to fight against the Canaanites, that dwelt in the mountain, and in the south, and in the valley.
text.NABRE: Afterward the Judahites went down to fight against the Canaanites who lived in the mountain region, in the Negeb, and in the foothills.
text.DRB: And afterward going down they fought against the Chananite, which dwelled in the mountains, and southward, & in the champion countries.

text.NRSV: Judah went against the Canaanites who lived in Hebron (the name of Hebron was formerly Kiriath-arba); and they defeated Sheshai and Ahiman and Talmai.
text.Rotherham: And Judah went against the Canaanites who were dwelling in Hebron, now, the name of Hebron, formerly, was Kiriath-arba,—and they smote Sheshai and Ahiman, and Talmai.
text.KJV: And Judah went against the Canaanites that dwelt in Hebron: (now the name of Hebron before was Kirjath–arba:) and they slew Sheshai, and Ahiman, and Talmai.
text.NABRE: Judah also marched against the Canaanites who lived in Hebron, which was formerly called Kiriath-arba, and defeated Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai.
text.DRB: And Judas going forward, against the Chananite, that dwelled in Hebron (the name whereof was before time Cariatharbe) struck Sesai, and Ahiman, and Tholmai:

text.NRSV: From there they went against the inhabitants of Debir (the name of Debir was formerly Kiriath-sepher).
text.Rotherham: And he went from thence, against the inhabitants of Debir,—now, the name of Debir, formerly, was Kiriath-sepher.
text.KJV: And from thence he went against the inhabitants of Debir: and the name of Debir before was Kirjath–sepher:
text.NABRE: They marched from there against the inhabitants of Debir, which was formerly called Kiriath-sepher.
text.DRB: and departing thence went to the inhabitants of Dabir, the old name whereof was Cariath Sepher, that is a city of letters.

text.NRSV: Then Caleb said, “Whoever attacks Kiriath-sepher and takes it, I will give him my daughter Achsah as wife.”
text.Rotherham: And Caleb said, He that smiteth Kiriath-sepher, and captureth it, I will give unto him Achsah my daughter, to wife.
text.KJV: And Caleb said, He that smiteth Kirjath–sepher, and taketh it, to him will I give Achsah my daughter to wife.
text.NABRE: Caleb said, “To the man who attacks Kiriath-sepher and captures it, I will give my daughter Achsah in marriage.”
text.DRB: And Caleb said: He that shall strike Cariath Sepher, and spoil it, I will give him Axa my daughter to wife.

text.NRSV: And Othniel son of Kenaz, Caleb’s younger brother, took it; and he gave him his daughter Achsah as wife.
text.Rotherham: Then Othniel, son of Kenaz, Caleb’s younger brother, captured it,—and he gave him Achsah his daughter, to wife.
text.KJV: And Othniel the son of Kenaz, Caleb’s younger brother, took it: and he gave him Achsah his daughter to wife.
text.NABRE: Othniel captured it, the son of Caleb’s younger brother Kenaz; so Caleb gave him his daughter Achsah in marriage.
text.DRB: And when Othoniel the son of Cenez, the younger brother of Caleb had taken it, he gave him Axa his daughter to wife.

text.NRSV: When she came to him, she urged him to ask her father for a field. As she dismounted from her donkey, Caleb said to her, “What do you wish?”
text.Rotherham: And it came pass, when she came, that she moved him to ask of her father a field, and, when she alighted from off the ass, Caleb said unto her—What aileth thee?
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when she came to him, that she moved him to ask of her father a field: and she lighted from off her ass; and Caleb said unto her, What wilt thou?
text.NABRE: When she came to him, she induced him to ask her father for some land. Then, as she alighted from the donkey, Caleb asked her, “What do you want?”
text.DRB: Whom going on her way her husband admonished to ask a field of her father. To whom, when she had sighed sitting on her ass, Caleb said: What aileth thee?

text.NRSV: She said to him, “Give me a present; since you have set me in the land of the Negeb, give me also Gulloth-mayim.” So Caleb gave her Upper Gulloth and Lower Gulloth.
text.Rotherham: And she said unto him—Give me a present; for, south land, hast thou given me, give me therefore pools of water. So Caleb gave her Upper-pools, and Lower-pools.
text.KJV: And she said unto him, Give me a blessing: for thou hast given me a south land; give me also springs of water. And Caleb gave her the upper springs and the nether springs.
text.NABRE: She answered, “Give me a present. Since you have put me in the land of the Negeb, give me pools of water.” So Caleb gave her what she wanted, both the upper and the lower pool.
text.DRB: But she answered. Give me a blessing, for a dry land thou hast given me: give me also a watery. Caleb therefore gave her a watery ground above, and watery beneath.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Hobab the Kenite, Moses’ father-in-law, went up with the people of Judah from the city of palms into the wilderness of Judah, which lies in the Negeb near Arad. Then they went and settled with the Amalekites.
text.Rotherham: Now, the sons of the Kenite, father-in-law of Moses, had come up from the city of palm-trees, with the sons of Judah, into the wilderness of Judah, which is in the south of Arad,—so they went and dwelt with the people.
text.KJV: And the children of the Kenite, Moses’ father in law, went up out of the city of palm trees with the children of Judah into the wilderness of Judah, which lieth in the south of Arad; and they went and dwelt among the people.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Hobab the Kenite, Moses’ father-in-law, came up with the Judahites from the City of Palms to the wilderness of Arad, which is in the Negeb, and they settled among the Amalekites.
text.DRB: And the children of the Cineite the cousin of Moyses went up from the city of palms, with the children of Judas into the desert of his lot, which is at the south side of Arad, & dwelt with him.

text.NRSV: Judah went with his brother Simeon, and they defeated the Canaanites who inhabited Zephath, and devoted it to destruction. So the city was called Hormah.
text.Rotherham: Then went Judah, with Simeon his brother, and they smote the Canaanites dwelling in Zephath,—and devoted it to destruction, and the name of the city was called Hormah.
text.KJV: And Judah went with Simeon his brother, and they slew the Canaanites that inhabited Zephath, and utterly destroyed it. And the name of the city was called Hormah.
text.NABRE: Then Judah went with his brother Simeon, and they defeated the Canaanites who lived in Zephath. They put the city under the ban and renamed it Hormah.
text.DRB: But Judas went with Simeon his brother, & together they struck the Chananite that dwelt in Sephaath, and slew him. And the name of the city was called, Horma, that is, Anathema.

text.NRSV: Judah took Gaza with its territory, Ashkelon with its territory, and Ekron with its territory.
text.Rotherham: And Judah captured Gaza with the boundaries thereof, and Ashkelon, with the boundaries thereof,—and Ekron, with the boundaries thereof.
text.KJV: Also Judah took Gaza with the coast thereof, and Askelon with the coast thereof, and Ekron with the coast thereof.
text.NABRE: Judah captured Gaza with its territory, Ashkelon with its territory, Ekron with its territory, and Ashdod with its territory.
text.DRB: And Judas took Gaza with the coasts thereof, & Ascalon, & Accaron with their bounds.

text.NRSV: The Lord was with Judah, and he took possession of the hill country, but could not drive out the inhabitants of the plain, because they had chariots of iron.
text.Rotherham: And it same to pass that Yahweh was with Judah, and he took possession of the hill country,—but did not dispossess the inhabitants of the vale, because they had, chariots of iron.
text.KJV: And the Lord was with Judah; and he drave out the inhabitants of the mountain; but could not drive out the inhabitants of the valley, because they had chariots of iron.
text.NABRE: The Lord was with Judah, so they gained possession of the mountain region. But they could not dispossess those who lived on the plain, because they had iron chariots.
text.DRB: And our Lord was with Judas, & he possessed the mountains: neither could he destroy the inhabitants of the valley, because they had many hooked chariots.

text.NRSV: Hebron was given to Caleb, as Moses had said; and he drove out from it the three sons of Anak.
text.Rotherham: So they gave unto Caleb Hebron, as spake Moses,—and he drave out from thence the three sons of Anak.
text.KJV: And they gave Hebron unto Caleb, as Moses said: and he expelled thence the three sons of Anak.
text.NABRE: As Moses had commanded, they gave Hebron to Caleb, who then drove the three sons of Anak away from there.
text.DRB: And they gave to Caleb Hebron, as Moyses had said, who destroyed out of it the three sons of Enac.

text.NRSV: But the Benjaminites did not drive out the Jebusites who lived in Jerusalem; so the Jebusites have lived in Jerusalem among the Benjaminites to this day.
text.Rotherham: But, the Jebusites dwelling in Jerusalem, the sons of Benjamin did not drive out,—but the Jebusites have dwelt with the sons of Benjamin, in Jerusalem, unto this day.
text.KJV: And the children of Benjamin did not drive out the Jebusites that inhabited Jerusalem; but the Jebusites dwell with the children of Benjamin in Jerusalem unto this day.
text.NABRE: As for the Jebusites dwelling in Jerusalem, the Benjaminites did not dispossess them, so that the Jebusites live with the Benjaminites in Jerusalem to the present day.
text.DRB: But the Jebusite the inhabiter of Jerusalem the children of Benjamin destroyed not: and the Jebusite dwelt with the children of Benjamin in Jerusalem until this present day.

text.NRSV: The house of Joseph also went up against Bethel; and the Lord was with them.
text.Rotherham: Then went up the house of Joseph—they also,—unto Bethel,—and, Yahweh, was with them.
text.KJV: And the house of Joseph, they also went up against Beth–el: and the Lord was with them.
text.NABRE: The house of Joseph, too, went up against Bethel, and the Lord was with them.
text.DRB: The house also of Joseph went up into Bethel, and our Lord was with them.

text.NRSV: The house of Joseph sent out spies to Bethel (the name of the city was formerly Luz).
text.Rotherham: And the house of Joseph sent to spy out Bethel,—now, the name of the city, formerly, was, Luz.
text.KJV: And the house of Joseph sent to descry Beth–el. (Now the name of the city before was Luz.)
text.NABRE: The house of Joseph reconnoitered Bethel, which formerly was called Luz.
text.DRB: For when they besieged the city, which before was called Luza,

text.NRSV: When the spies saw a man coming out of the city, they said to him, “Show us the way into the city, and we will deal kindly with you.”
text.Rotherham: So the watchers saw a man, coming forth from the city,—and they said unto him—Shew us, we pray thee, the way to get into the city, and we will deal with thee, in lovingkindness.
text.KJV: And the spies saw a man come forth out of the city, and they said unto him, Shew us, we pray thee, the entrance into the city, and we will shew thee mercy.
text.NABRE: The scouts saw a man coming out of the city and said to him, “Tell us the way into the city, and we will show you mercy.”
text.DRB: they saw a man coming out of the city, and said to him: Shew us the entrance of the city, and we will shew thee mercy.

text.NRSV: So he showed them the way into the city; and they put the city to the sword, but they let the man and all his family go.
text.Rotherham: And he shewed them the way to get into the city, and they smote the city, with the edge of the sword,—but, the man and all his family, they let go.
text.KJV: And when he shewed them the entrance into the city, they smote the city with the edge of the sword; but they let go the man and all his family.
text.NABRE: He showed them the way into the city, and they put the city to the sword; but they let the man and his whole clan go free.
text.DRB: Who when he had shewed them, they struck the city in the edge of the sword: but that man, & all his kindred they dismissed.

text.NRSV: So the man went to the land of the Hittites and built a city, and named it Luz; that is its name to this day.
text.Rotherham: And the man went into the land of the Hittites,—and built a city, and called the name thereof, Luz, that, is the name thereof, unto this day.
text.KJV: And the man went into the land of the Hittites, and built a city, and called the name thereof Luz: which is the name thereof unto this day.
text.NABRE: The man then went to the land of the Hittites, where he built a city and called it Luz, which is its name to this day.
text.DRB: Who being dismissed, went into the Land of the Hetthims, & built there a city, & called it Luza: which is so called until this present day.

text.NRSV: Manasseh did not drive out the inhabitants of Beth-shean and its villages, or Taanach and its villages, or the inhabitants of Dor and its villages, or the inhabitants of Ibleam and its villages, or the inhabitants of Megiddo and its villages; but the Canaanites continued to live in that land.
text.Rotherham: But Manasseh took not possession of Beth-shean and her towns, nor of Taanach and her towns, nor dispossessed the inhabitants of Dor and her towns, nor the inhabitants of Ibleam, and her towns, nor the inhabitants of Megiddo, and her towns,—but the Canaanites were determined to remain in this land;
text.KJV: Neither did Manasseh drive out the inhabitants of Beth–shean and her towns, nor Taanach and her towns, nor the inhabitants of Dor and her towns, nor the inhabitants of Ibleam and her towns, nor the inhabitants of Megiddo and her towns: but the Canaanites would dwell in that land.
text.NABRE: Manasseh did not take possession of Beth-shean with its towns or of Taanach with its towns. Nor did they dispossess the inhabitants of Dor and its towns, those of Ibleam and its towns, or those of Megiddo and its towns. The Canaanites continued to live in this district.
text.DRB: Manasses also destroyed not Bethsan, and Thanac with their little towns, and the inhabitants of Dor, and Jeblaam, and Mageddo with their little towns. And the Chananeite began to dwell with them.

text.NRSV: When Israel grew strong, they put the Canaanites to forced labor, but did not in fact drive them out.
text.Rotherham: when Israel, however, had waxed strong, they put the Canaanites under tribute,—though they, dispossessed, them not.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when Israel was strong, that they put the Canaanites to tribute, and did not utterly drive them out.
text.NABRE: When Israel grew stronger, they conscripted the Canaanites as laborers, but did not actually drive them out.
text.DRB: But after that Israel was waxen strong he made them tributaries, and would not destroy them.

text.NRSV: And Ephraim did not drive out the Canaanites who lived in Gezer; but the Canaanites lived among them in Gezer.
text.Rotherham: And, Ephraim, dispossessed not the Canaanites who were dwelling in Gezer,—so the Canaanites remained in their midst, in Gezer.
text.KJV: Neither did Ephraim drive out the Canaanites that dwelt in Gezer; but the Canaanites dwelt in Gezer among them.
text.NABRE: Ephraim did not drive out the Canaanites living in Gezer, and so the Canaanites lived among them in Gezer.
text.DRB: Ephraim also killed not the Chananite, that dwelt in Gazer, but dwelt with him.

text.NRSV: Zebulun did not drive out the inhabitants of Kitron, or the inhabitants of Nahalol; but the Canaanites lived among them, and became subject to forced labor.
text.Rotherham: Zebulun, dispossessed not the inhabitants of Kitron, nor the inhabitants of Nahalol,—but the Canaanites remained in their midst, and came under tribute.
text.KJV: Neither did Zebulun drive out the inhabitants of Kitron, nor the inhabitants of Nahalol; but the Canaanites dwelt among them, and became tributaries.
text.NABRE: Nor did Zebulun dispossess the inhabitants of Kitron or those of Nahalol; the Canaanites lived among them and became forced laborers.
text.DRB: Zabulon destroyed not the inhabitants of Cetron, and Naalol: but the Chananite dwelt in the midst of him, & was made tributary to him.

text.NRSV: Asher did not drive out the inhabitants of Acco, or the inhabitants of Sidon, or of Ahlab, or of Achzib, or of Helbah, or of Aphik, or of Rehob;
text.Rotherham: Asher, dispossessed not the inhabitants of Acco, nor the inhabitants of Zidon,—nor of Ahlab, nor of Achzib, nor of Helbah, nor of Aphik, nor of Rehob;
text.KJV: Neither did Asher drive out the inhabitants of Accho, nor the inhabitants of Zidon, nor of Ahlab, nor of Achzib, nor of Helbah, nor of Aphik, nor of Rehob:
text.NABRE: Nor did Asher dispossess the inhabitants of Acco or those of Sidon, or take possession of Mahaleb, Achzib, Helbah, Aphik, or Rehob.
text.DRB: Aser also destroyed not the inhabitants of Accho, & of Sidon, of Ahalab, and Achazib, & Helba, & Aphec, & Rohob:

text.NRSV: but the Asherites lived among the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land; for they did not drive them out.
text.Rotherham: but the Asherites dwelt in the midst of the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land,—for they dispossessed them not.
text.KJV: But the Asherites dwelt among the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land: for they did not drive them out.
text.NABRE: So the Asherites settled among the Canaanite inhabitants of the land, for they had not dispossessed them.
text.DRB: and he dwelt in the midst of the Chananite the inhabiter of that Land, neither did he kill them.

text.NRSV: Naphtali did not drive out the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh, or the inhabitants of Beth-anath, but lived among the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land; nevertheless the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh and of Beth-anath became subject to forced labor for them.
text.Rotherham: Naphtali, dispossessed not the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh nor the inhabitants of Beth-anath, but they dwelt in the midst of the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land,—and, the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh, and of Beth-anath, became theirs, under tribute.
text.KJV: Neither did Naphtali drive out the inhabitants of Beth–shemesh, nor the inhabitants of Beth–anath; but he dwelt among the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land: nevertheless the inhabitants of Beth–shemesh and of Beth–anath became tributaries unto them.
text.NABRE: Nor did Naphtali drive out the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh or those of Beth-anath. They settled among the Canaanite inhabitants of the land and the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh and Beth-anath became forced laborers for them.
text.DRB: Nephthali also destroyed not the inhabitants of Bethsames, & Bethanath: and he dwelt in the midst of the Chananite the inhabiter of the Land, and the Bethsamites & Bethanites were tributaries to him.

text.NRSV: The Amorites pressed the Danites back into the hill country; they did not allow them to come down to the plain.
text.Rotherham: And the Amorites forced the sons of Dan into the hill country,—for they suffered them not to come down into the vale;
text.KJV: And the Amorites forced the children of Dan into the mountain: for they would not suffer them to come down to the valley:
text.NABRE: The Amorites hemmed in the Danites in the mountain region, not permitting them to come down onto the plain.
text.DRB: And the Amorrhite straitened the children of Dan in the mountain, and gave them not place to go down to the plain:

text.NRSV: The Amorites continued to live in Har-heres, in Aijalon, and in Shaalbim, but the hand of the house of Joseph rested heavily on them, and they became subject to forced labor.
text.Rotherham: and, though the Amorites were determined to remain in the hill country of Heres, in Aijalon, and in Shaalbim, yet was the hand of the house of Joseph heavy, so that they came under tribute.
text.KJV: But the Amorites would dwell in mount Heres in Aijalon, and in Shaalbim: yet the hand of the house of Joseph prevailed, so that they became tributaries.
text.NABRE: So the Amorites continued to live in Harheres, Aijalon, and Shaalbim, but as the power of the house of Joseph grew, they were conscripted as laborers.
text.DRB: and he dwelt in the mount Hares which is interpreted shells, in Aialon and Salebim. And the hand of the house of Joseph was aggravated, and he became tributary to him.

text.NRSV: The border of the Amorites ran from the ascent of Akrabbim, from Sela and upward.
text.Rotherham: And, the boundary of the Amorites, was from the ascent of Akrabbim, from Sela and upwards.
text.KJV: And the coast of the Amorites was from the going up to Akrabbim, from the rock, and upward.
text.NABRE: The territory of the Amorites extended from the Akrabbim pass, from Sela and upward.
text.DRB: And the border of the Amorrhite was from the Ascent of the Scorpion, the rock, and the higher places.

text.NRSV: Now the angel of the Lord went up from Gilgal to Bochim, and said, “I brought you up from Egypt, and brought you into the land that I had promised to your ancestors. I said, ‘I will never break my covenant with you.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh came up from Gilgal, unto Bochim. And he said—I led you up out of Egypt, and brought you into the land, which I had sworn unto your fathers, And I said, I will not break my covenant with you, to times age-abiding;
text.KJV: And an angel of the Lord came up from Gilgal to Bochim, and said, I made you to go up out of Egypt, and have brought you unto the land which I sware unto your fathers; and I said, I will never break my covenant with you.
text.NABRE: A messenger of the Lord went up from Gilgal to Bochim and said, I brought you up from Egypt and led you into the land which I promised on oath to your ancestors. I said, I will never break my covenant with you,
text.DRB: And the Angel of our Lord went up from Galgal to the place of weepers, and said: I brought you out of Ægypt, and have brought you into the Land, for the which I sware to your fathers: and I promised that I would not make frustrate my covenant with you for ever:

text.NRSV: For your part, do not make a covenant with the inhabitants of this land; tear down their altars.’ But you have not obeyed my command. See what you have done!
text.Rotherham: But, ye, must not solemnise a covenant with the inhabitants of this land, Their altars, must ye tear down,—But ye have not hearkened unto my voice, what is this ye have done?
text.KJV: And ye shall make no league with the inhabitants of this land; ye shall throw down their altars: but ye have not obeyed my voice: why have ye done this?
text.NABRE: but you must not make a covenant with the inhabitants of this land; you must pull down their altars. But you did not listen to me. Look what you have done!
text.DRB: only so that you should not make a league with the inhabitants of this Land, but should overthrow their altars: and you would not hear my voice. Why have you done this?

text.NRSV: So now I say, I will not drive them out before you; but they shall become adversaries to you, and their gods shall be a snare to you.”
text.Rotherham: Wherefore also I said, I will not drive them out from before you,—but they shall be your adversaries, and, their gods, shall become unto you a snare.
text.KJV: Wherefore I also said, I will not drive them out from before you; but they shall be as thorns in your sides, and their gods shall be a snare unto you.
text.NABRE: For I also said, I will not clear them out of your way; they will become traps for you, and their gods a snare for you.
text.DRB: For the which cause I would not destroy them from before your face: that you may have enemies, and their gods may be a ruin unto you.

text.NRSV: When the angel of the Lord spoke these words to all the Israelites, the people lifted up their voices and wept.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the messenger of Yahweh had spoken these words unto all the sons of Israel, that the people lifted up their voice, and wept.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when the angel of the Lord spake these words unto all the children of Israel, that the people lifted up their voice, and wept.
text.NABRE: When the messenger of the Lord had spoken these things to all the Israelites, the people wept aloud.
text.DRB: And when the Angel of our Lord spake these words to all the children of Israel, they lifted up their voice, and wept.

text.NRSV: So they named that place Bochim, and there they sacrificed to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So they called the name of that place, Bochim, and offered sacrifice there, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And they called the name of that place Bochim: and they sacrificed there unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: They named that place Bochim, and they offered sacrifice there to the Lord.
text.DRB: And the name of that place was called, the place of weepers, or of tears: and there they immolated hosts to our Lord.

text.NRSV: When Joshua dismissed the people, the Israelites all went to their own inheritances to take possession of the land.
text.Rotherham: Now, when Joshua had let the people go, the sons of Israel went their way, every man to his inheritance, to take possession of the land.
text.KJV: And when Joshua had let the people go, the children of Israel went every man unto his inheritance to possess the land.
text.NABRE: Then Joshua dismissed the people, and the Israelites went, each to their own heritage, to take possession of the land.
text.DRB: Josue therefore dismissed the people, and the children of Israel went every one into his possession, to obtain it:

text.NRSV: The people worshiped the Lord all the days of Joshua, and all the days of the elders who outlived Joshua, who had seen all the great work that the Lord had done for Israel.
text.Rotherham: And the people served Yahweh all the days of Joshua,—and all the days of the elders who outlived Joshua, who had seen all the great work of Yahweh which he had wrought for Israel.
text.KJV: And the people served the Lord all the days of Joshua, and all the days of the elders that outlived Joshua, who had seen all the great works of the Lord, that he did for Israel.
text.NABRE: The people served the Lord during the entire lifetime of Joshua, and of those elders who outlived Joshua and who had seen all the great work the Lord had done for Israel.
text.DRB: and they served our Lord all his days, and the days of the ancients, of them that lived a long time after him, and knew all the works of our Lord, which he had done with Israel.

text.NRSV: Joshua son of Nun, the servant of the Lord, died at the age of one hundred ten years.
text.Rotherham: So then Joshua, the son of Nun, the servant of Yahweh, died,—being a hundred and ten years old.
text.KJV: And Joshua the son of Nun, the servant of the Lord, died, being an hundred and ten years old.
text.NABRE: Joshua, son of Nun, the servant of the Lord, died at the age of a hundred and ten,
text.DRB: And Josue the son of Nun, the servant of our Lord, died, being an hundred and ten years old,

text.NRSV: So they buried him within the bounds of his inheritance in Timnath-heres, in the hill country of Ephraim, north of Mount Gaash.
text.Rotherham: And they buried him within the bounds of his inheritance, in Timnath-heres, in the hill country of Ephraim,—on the north of Mount Gaash.
text.KJV: And they buried him in the border of his inheritance in Timnath–heres, in the mount of Ephraim, on the north side of the hill Gaash.
text.NABRE: and they buried him within the borders of his heritage at Timnath-heres in the mountain region of Ephraim north of Mount Gaash.
text.DRB: and they buried him in the borders of his possession in Thamnathsare in the mount of Ephraim, on the North side of mount Gaas.

text.NRSV: Moreover, that whole generation was gathered to their ancestors, and another generation grew up after them, who did not know the Lord or the work that he had done for Israel.
text.Rotherham: All that generation also, were gathered unto their fathers,—and there arose another generation, after them, who had not known Yahweh, nor even the work which he had wrought for Israel.
text.KJV: And also all that generation were gathered unto their fathers: and there arose another generation after them, which knew not the Lord, nor yet the works which he had done for Israel.
text.NABRE: When the rest of that generation were also gathered to their ancestors, and a later generation arose that did not know the Lord or the work he had done for Israel,
text.DRB: And all that generation was gathered to their fathers: and there rose others, that knew not our Lord, and the works which he had done with Israel.

text.NRSV: Then the Israelites did what was evil in the sight of the Lord and worshiped the Baals;
text.Rotherham: So the sons of Israel did the thing that was wicked in the sight of Yahweh,—and served the Baals;
text.KJV: And the children of Israel did evil in the sight of the Lord, and served Baalim:
text.NABRE: the Israelites did what was evil in the sight of the Lord. They served the Baals,
text.DRB: And the children of Israel did evil in the sight of our Lord, and served Baalim.

text.NRSV: and they abandoned the Lord, the God of their ancestors, who had brought them out of the land of Egypt; they followed other gods, from among the gods of the peoples who were all around them, and bowed down to them; and they provoked the Lord to anger.
text.Rotherham: and forsook Yahweh, the God of their fathers, who brought them up out of the land of Egypt, and went after other gods, from among the gods of the peoples who were round about them, and bowed themselves down unto them,—and angered Yahweh.
text.KJV: And they forsook the Lord God of their fathers, which brought them out of the land of Egypt, and followed other gods, of the gods of the people that were round about them, and bowed themselves unto them, and provoked the Lord to anger.
text.NABRE: and abandoned the Lord, the God of their ancestors, the one who had brought them out of the land of Egypt. They followed other gods, the gods of the peoples around them, and bowed down to them, and provoked the Lord.
text.DRB: And they left our Lord the God of their fathers, that had brought them out of the Land of Ægypt: and followed strange gods, and the gods of the peoples, that dwelt round about them, and adored them: and they provoked our Lord to anger,

text.NRSV: They abandoned the Lord, and worshipped Baal and the Astartes.
text.Rotherham: Yea, they forsook Yahweh,—and did service unto the Baal and unto Ashtaroth.
text.KJV: And they forsook the Lord, and served Baal and Ashtaroth.
text.NABRE: Because they had abandoned the Lord and served Baal and the Astartes,
text.DRB: leaving him, and serving Baal and Astaroth.

text.NRSV: So the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel, and he gave them over to plunderers who plundered them, and he sold them into the power of their enemies all around, so that they could no longer withstand their enemies.
text.Rotherham: Then kindled the anger of Yahweh upon Israel, and he delivered them into the hand of spoilers, who plundered them,—and he sold them into the hand of their enemies round about, so that they could no longer stand before their enemies.
text.KJV: And the anger of the Lord was hot against Israel, and he delivered them into the hands of spoilers that spoiled them, and he sold them into the hands of their enemies round about, so that they could not any longer stand before their enemies.
text.NABRE: the anger of the Lord flared up against Israel, and he delivered them into the power of plunderers who despoiled them. He sold them into the power of the enemies around them, and they were no longer able to withstand their enemies.
text.DRB: And our Lord being wrath against Israel, delivered them into the hands of ransackers: who took them and sold them to the enemies, that dwelt round about: neither could they resist their adversaries:

text.NRSV: Whenever they marched out, the hand of the Lord was against them to bring misfortune, as the Lord had warned them and sworn to them; and they were in great distress.
text.Rotherham: Whithersoever they went out, the hand of Yahweh was found to be against them for misfortune, as spake Yahweh, and as sware Yahweh unto them,—and they were distressed exceedingly.
text.KJV: Whithersoever they went out, the hand of the Lord was against them for evil, as the Lord had said, and as the Lord had sworn unto them: and they were greatly distressed.
text.NABRE: Whenever they marched out, the hand of the Lord turned against them, as the Lord had said, and as the Lord had sworn to them; and they were in great distress.
text.DRB: but whither soever they had meant to go, the hand of our Lord was upon them, as he spake, and sware to them: and they were vehemently afflicted.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord raised up judges, who delivered them out of the power of those who plundered them.
text.Rotherham: And, though Yahweh raised up judges,—who saved them out of the hand of them that plundered them,
text.KJV: Nevertheless the Lord raised up judges, which delivered them out of the hand of those that spoiled them.
text.NABRE: But the Lord raised up judges to save them from the power of their plunderers;
text.DRB: And our Lord raised up Judges, that should deliver them from the hands of the wasters: but neither would they hear them,

text.NRSV: Yet they did not listen even to their judges; for they lusted after other gods and bowed down to them. They soon turned aside from the way in which their ancestors had walked, who had obeyed the commandments of the Lord; they did not follow their example.
text.Rotherham: yet, even unto their judges, did thy not hearken, for they went unchastely astray after other gods, and bowed themselves down to them,—they turned aside speedily out of the way wherein their fathers who hearkened unto the commandments of Yahweh, walked, they did not so.
text.KJV: And yet they would not hearken unto their judges, but they went a whoring after other gods, and bowed themselves unto them: they turned quickly out of the way which their fathers walked in, obeying the commandments of the Lord; but they did not so.
text.NABRE: but they did not listen to their judges either, for they prostituted themselves by following other gods, bowing down to them. They were quick to stray from the way their ancestors had taken, who obeyed the commandments of the Lord; but these did not.
text.DRB: fornicating with strange gods, and adoring them. They did quickly forsake the way, in the which their fathers had gone: and hearing the commandments of our Lord, they did all things contrary.

text.NRSV: Whenever the Lord raised up judges for them, the Lord was with the judge, and he delivered them from the hand of their enemies all the days of the judge; for the Lord would be moved to pity by their groaning because of those who persecuted and oppressed them.
text.Rotherham: And, when Yahweh raised them up judges, then was Yahweh with the judges, and saved them out of the hand of their enemies, all the days of the judge,—for Yahweh was grieved at their outcry, because of them who oppressed them, and ill-treated them.
text.KJV: And when the Lord raised them up judges, then the Lord was with the judge, and delivered them out of the hand of their enemies all the days of the judge: for it repented the Lord because of their groanings by reason of them that oppressed them and vexed them.
text.NABRE: When the Lord raised up judges for them, he would be with the judge and save them from the power of their enemies as long as the judge lived. The Lord would change his mind when they groaned in their affliction under their oppressors.
text.DRB: And when our Lord raised up Judges, in their days he was moved with mercy, and heard the groanings of the afflicted, and delivered them from the slaughter of the wasters.

text.NRSV: But whenever the judge died, they would relapse and behave worse than their ancestors, following other gods, worshiping them and bowing down to them. They would not drop any of their practices or their stubborn ways.
text.Rotherham: But, when the judge was dead, they again broke faith more than their fathers, by going their way after other gods, by serving them, and bowing themselves down to them,—they ceased not from their doings nor from their stubborn way.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when the judge was dead, that they returned, and corrupted themselves more than their fathers, in following other gods to serve them, and to bow down unto them; they ceased not from their own doings, nor from their stubborn way.
text.NABRE: But when the judge died, they would again do worse than their ancestors, following other gods, serving and bowing down to them, relinquishing none of their evil practices or stubborn ways.
text.DRB: But after the Judge was dead, they returned, and did much worse things than their fathers had done, following strange gods, serving them, and adoring them. They left not their inventions, and the very hard way, by which they were accustomed to walk.

text.NRSV: So the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel; and he said, “Because this people have transgressed my covenant that I commanded their ancestors, and have not obeyed my voice,
text.Rotherham: So then the anger of Yahweh kindled upon Israel, and he said—Forasmuch as this nation have transgressed my covenant, which I commanded their fathers, and have not hearkened unto my voice,
text.KJV: And the anger of the Lord was hot against Israel; and he said, Because that this people hath transgressed my covenant which I commanded their fathers, and have not hearkened unto my voice;
text.NABRE: The anger of the Lord flared up against Israel, and he said: Because this nation has transgressed my covenant, which I enjoined on their ancestors, and has not listened to me,
text.DRB: And the fury of our Lord was angry against Israel, & said: Because this nation hath made my covenant frustrate, which I had made with their fathers, and hath contemned my voice:

text.NRSV: I will no longer drive out before them any of the nations that Joshua left when he died.”
text.Rotherham: I also, will no further dispossess a man from before them,—from among the nations which Joshua left remaining, when he died;
text.KJV: I also will not henceforth drive out any from before them of the nations which Joshua left when he died:
text.NABRE: I for my part will not clear away for them any more of the nations Joshua left when he died.
text.DRB: I also will not destroy the nations which Josue did let alone, and died:

text.NRSV: In order to test Israel, whether or not they would take care to walk in the way of the Lord as their ancestors did,
text.Rotherham: that I may, by them, put Israel to the proof,—whether they are going to be observant of the way of Yahweh, to walk therein as their fathers observed it, or not.
text.KJV: That through them I may prove Israel, whether they will keep the way of the Lord to walk therein, as their fathers did keep it, or not.
text.NABRE: They will be made to test Israel, to see whether or not they will keep to the way of the Lord and continue in it as their ancestors did.
text.DRB: that in them I may try Israel, whether they will keep the way of the Lord, and walk in it, as their fathers kept it, or not.

text.NRSV: the Lord had left those nations, not driving them out at once, and had not handed them over to Joshua.
text.Rotherham: Thus then Yahweh left these nations, not dispossessing them speedily,—neither had he delivered them into the hand of Joshua
text.KJV: Therefore the Lord left those nations, without driving them out hastily; neither delivered he them into the hand of Joshua.
text.NABRE: Therefore the Lord allowed these nations to remain instead of expelling them immediately. He had not delivered them into the power of Joshua.
text.DRB: Our Lord therefore left all these nations, and would not quickly overthrow them, neither delivered them into the hands of Josue.

text.NRSV: Now these are the nations that the Lord left to test all those in Israel who had no experience of any war in Canaan
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the nations which Yahweh left, that he might, by them, put Israel to the proof,—all who had not known any of the wars of Canaan;
text.KJV: Now these are the nations which the Lord left, to prove Israel by them, even as many of Israel as had not known all the wars of Canaan;
text.NABRE: These are the nations the Lord allowed to remain, so that through them he might test Israel, all those who had not experienced any of the Canaanite wars—
text.DRB: These are the Nations, which our Lord left, that in them he might instruct Israel, and all that had not known the wars of the Chananites:

text.NRSV: (it was only that successive generations of Israelites might know war, to teach those who had no experience of it before):
text.Rotherham: that the generations of the sons of Israel might certainly get to know, by being taught to make war,—such, at least, as aforetime knew nothing thereof:—
text.KJV: Only that the generations of the children of Israel might know, to teach them war, at the least such as before knew nothing thereof;
text.NABRE: to teach warfare to those generations of Israelites who had never experienced it:
text.DRB: that afterward their children might learn to fight with their enemies, and to be accustomed to war:

text.NRSV: the five lords of the Philistines, and all the Canaanites, and the Sidonians, and the Hivites who lived on Mount Lebanon, from Mount Baal-hermon as far as Lebo-hamath.
text.Rotherham: five lords of the Philistines, and all the Canaanites, and the Zidonians, and the Hivites dwelling in Mount Lebanon,—from Mount Baal-hermon as far as the entering in of Hamath.
text.KJV: Namely, five lords of the Philistines, and all the Canaanites, and the Sidonians, and the Hivites that dwelt in mount Lebanon, from mount Baal–hermon unto the entering in of Hamath.
text.NABRE: the five lords of the Philistines, and all the Canaanites, the Sidonians, and the Hivites who lived in the mountain region of the Lebanon between Baal-hermon and Lebo-hamath.
text.DRB: the five Princes of the Philisthiims, and the Chananite, and Sidonian, and Heveite, that dwelt in mount Libanus, from mount Baal Hermon to the entering into Emath.

text.NRSV: They were for the testing of Israel, to know whether Israel would obey the commandments of the Lord, which he commanded their ancestors by Moses.
text.Rotherham: So then [these] were [left], that, by them, he might put Israel to the proof,—to know whether they would hearken unto the commandments of Yahweh which he commanded their fathers by the hand of Moses.
text.KJV: And they were to prove Israel by them, to know whether they would hearken unto the commandments of the Lord, which he commanded their fathers by the hand of Moses.
text.NABRE: These served as a test for Israel, to know whether they would obey the commandments the Lord had enjoined on their ancestors through Moses.
text.DRB: And he left them, that in them he might try Israel, whether they would hear the commandments of our Lord, which he had commanded their fathers by the hand of Moyses, or not.

text.NRSV: So the Israelites lived among the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites;
text.Rotherham: And thus, the sons of Israel, dwelt in the midst of the Canaanites,—the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Perizzites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites;
text.KJV: And the children of Israel dwelt among the Canaanites, Hittites, and Amorites, and Perizzites, and Hivites, and Jebusites:
text.NABRE: So the Israelites settled among the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites.
text.DRB: Therefore the children of Israel dwelt in the midst of the Chananite, and Hetheite, and Amorrhite, and Pherezite, and Heveite, and Jebusite:

text.NRSV: and they took their daughters as wives for themselves, and their own daughters they gave to their sons; and they worshiped their gods.
text.Rotherham: and took their daughters to be their wives, their own daughters moreover, gave they unto their sons, and they served their gods.
text.KJV: And they took their daughters to be their wives, and gave their daughters to their sons, and served their gods.
text.NABRE: They took their daughters in marriage, and gave their own daughters to their sons in marriage, and served their gods.
text.DRB: and they took their daughters to wives, and themselves gave their own daughters to their sons, and served their gods.

text.NRSV: The Israelites did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, forgetting the Lord their God, and worshiping the Baals and the Asherahs.
text.Rotherham: Thus did the sons of Israel the thing that was wicked in the sight of Yahweh, and forgat Yahweh their God,—and served the Baals and the Asherahs.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel did evil in the sight of the Lord, and forgat the Lord their God, and served Baalim and the groves.
text.NABRE: Then the Israelites did what was evil in the sight of the Lord; they forgot the Lord, their God, and served the Baals and the Asherahs,
text.DRB: And they did evil in the sight of our Lord, and forgot their God, serving Baalim and Astaroth.

text.NRSV: Therefore the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel, and he sold them into the hand of King Cushan-rishathaim of Aram-naharaim; and the Israelites served Cushan-rishathaim eight years.
text.Rotherham: Then kindled the anger of Yahweh upon Israel, and he sold them into the hand of Chushan-rishathaim, king of Mesopotamia,—and the sons of Israel served Chushan-rishathaim, eight years.
text.KJV: Therefore the anger of the Lord was hot against Israel, and he sold them into the hand of Chushan–rishathaim king of Mesopotamia: and the children of Israel served Chushan–rishathaim eight years.
text.NABRE: and the anger of the Lord flared up against them. He sold them into the power of Cushan-rishathaim, king of Aram Naharaim; and the Israelites served Cushan-rishathaim for eight years.
text.DRB: And our Lord being wrath against Israel, delivered them into the hands of Chusan Rasathaim the king of Mesopotamia, and they served him eight years.

text.NRSV: But when the Israelites cried out to the Lord, the Lord raised up a deliverer for the Israelites, who delivered them, Othniel son of Kenaz, Caleb’s younger brother.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel made outcry unto Yahweh, so Yahweh raised up a saviour unto the sons of Israel, who saved them,—even Othniel son of Kenaz, Caleb’s younger brother.
text.KJV: And when the children of Israel cried unto the Lord, the Lord raised up a deliverer to the children of Israel, who delivered them, even Othniel the son of Kenaz, Caleb’s younger brother.
text.NABRE: But when the Israelites cried out to the Lord, he raised up a savior for them, to save them. It was Othniel, son of Caleb’s younger brother Kenaz.
text.DRB: And they cried to our Lord: who raised them up a saviour, & delivered them, to wit, Othoniel the son of Cenez, the younger brother of Caleb:

text.NRSV: The spirit of the Lord came upon him, and he judged Israel; he went out to war, and the Lord gave King Cushan-rishathaim of Aram into his hand; and his hand prevailed over Cushan-rishathaim.
text.Rotherham: And the Spirit of Yahweh came upon him, and he judged Israel, and went out to war, and Yahweh delivered into his hand, Chushan-rishathaim, king of Syria,—and his hand prevailed over Chushan-rishathaim.
text.KJV: And the Spirit of the Lord came upon him, and he judged Israel, and went out to war: and the Lord delivered Chushan–rishathaim king of Mesopotamia into his hand; and his hand prevailed against Chushan–rishathaim.
text.NABRE: The spirit of the Lord came upon him, and he judged Israel. When he marched out to war, the Lord delivered Cushan-rishathaim, king of Aram, into his power, and his hold on Cushan-rishathaim was firm.
text.DRB: and the Spirit of our Lord was in him, and he judged Israel. And he went forth to fight, and our Lord delivered into his hands Chusan Rasathaim the king of Syria, and oppressed him.

text.NRSV: So the land had rest forty years. Then Othniel son of Kenaz died.
text.Rotherham: And the land had rest forty years,—and Othniel son of Kenaz, died.
text.KJV: And the land had rest forty years. And Othniel the son of Kenaz died.
text.NABRE: So the land was at rest for forty years, until Othniel, son of Kenaz, died.
text.DRB: And the land rested forty years, and Othoniel the son of Cenez died.

text.NRSV: The Israelites again did what was evil in the sight of the Lord; and the Lord strengthened King Eglon of Moab against Israel, because they had done what was evil in the sight of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel again did the thing that was wicked, in the sight of Yahweh,—and Yahweh emboldened Eglon, king of Moab, against Israel, because they did the thing that was wicked in the sight of Yahweh;
text.KJV: And the children of Israel did evil again in the sight of the Lord: and the Lord strengthened Eglon the king of Moab against Israel, because they had done evil in the sight of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Ehud. Again the Israelites did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, so he strengthened Eglon, king of Moab, against Israel because they did what was evil in the sight of the Lord.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel added to do evil in the sight of our Lord: who strengthened against them Eglon the king of Moab: because they did evil in his sight.

text.NRSV: In alliance with the Ammonites and the Amalekites, he went and defeated Israel; and they took possession of the city of palms.
text.Rotherham: and he gathered unto him, the sons of Ammon, and Amalek,—and came and smote Israel, and took possession of the city of palm-trees.
text.KJV: And he gathered unto him the children of Ammon and Amalek, and went and smote Israel, and possessed the city of palm trees.
text.NABRE: Taking the Ammonites and Amalek as allies, he went and defeated Israel, taking possession of the City of Palms.
text.DRB: And he joined to him the children of Ammon, and Amalec: and he went and struck Israel, and possessed the city of palms.

text.NRSV: So the Israelites served King Eglon of Moab eighteen years.
text.Rotherham: So the sons of Israel served Eglon king of Moab, eighteen years.
text.KJV: So the children of Israel served Eglon the king of Moab eighteen years.
text.NABRE: So the Israelites served Eglon, king of Moab, for eighteen years.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel served Eglon the king of Moab eighteen years:

text.NRSV: But when the Israelites cried out to the Lord, the Lord raised up for them a deliverer, Ehud son of Gera, the Benjaminite, a left-handed man. The Israelites sent tribute by him to King Eglon of Moab.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel made outcry unto Yahweh, and Yahweh raised up unto them a saviour, Ehud son of Gera, a Benjamite, a man left-handed,—and the sons of Israel sent, by his hand, a present, unto Eglon king of Moab.
text.KJV: But when the children of Israel cried unto the Lord, the Lord raised them up a deliverer, Ehud the son of Gera, a Benjamite, a man lefthanded: and by him the children of Israel sent a present unto Eglon the king of Moab.
text.NABRE: But when the Israelites cried out to the Lord, he raised up for them a savior, Ehud, son of Gera, a Benjaminite who was left-handed. The Israelites would send their tribute to Eglon, king of Moab, by him.
text.DRB: and afterward they cried to our Lord: who raised up unto them a saviour called Aod, the son of Gera, the son of Jemini, who used both hands for the right. And the children of Israel sent by him presents to Eglon the king of Moab.

text.NRSV: Ehud made for himself a sword with two edges, a cubit in length; and he fastened it on his right thigh under his clothes.
text.Rotherham: So Ehud made himself a sword which had two edges, a cubit in length,—and girded it under his raiment, upon his right thigh.
text.KJV: But Ehud made him a dagger which had two edges, of a cubit length; and he did gird it under his raiment upon his right thigh.
text.NABRE: Ehud made himself a two-edged dagger a foot long, and strapped it under his clothes on his right thigh.
text.DRB: Who made himself a two edged sword, having in the midst a haft in length the palm of a hand, and was girded therewith under his cassock on the right thigh.

text.NRSV: Then he presented the tribute to King Eglon of Moab. Now Eglon was a very fat man.
text.Rotherham: So he offered the present unto Eglon, king of Moab,—now, Eglon, was an exceedingly fat man.
text.KJV: And he brought the present unto Eglon king of Moab: and Eglon was a very fat man.
text.NABRE: He presented the tribute to Eglon, king of Moab; now Eglon was a very fat man.
text.DRB: And he presented the gifts to Eglon the king of Moab. And Eglon was exceeding gross.

text.NRSV: When Ehud had finished presenting the tribute, he sent the people who carried the tribute on their way.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, when he had made an end of offering the present, that he sent away the people who had been bearing the present;
text.KJV: And when he had made an end to offer the present, he sent away the people that bare the present.
text.NABRE: When he had finished presenting the tribute, he dismissed the troops who had carried the tribute.
text.DRB: And when he had presented the gifts unto him, he brought his fellows on the way that came with him.

text.NRSV: But he himself turned back at the sculptured stones near Gilgal, and said, “I have a secret message for you, O king.” So the king said, “Silence!” and all his attendants went out from his presence.
text.Rotherham: but, he himself, turned back from the images that were by Gilgal, and said, A secret word, have I, unto thee, O king! And he said—Silence! Thereupon went out from his presence all who had been standing near him.
text.KJV: But he himself turned again from the quarries that were by Gilgal, and said, I have a secret errand unto thee, O king: who said, Keep silence. And all that stood by him went out from him.
text.NABRE: But he himself turned back at the sculptured stones near Gilgal, and said, “I have a secret message for you, O king.” And the king said, “Silence!” Then when all his attendants had left his presence,
text.DRB: And returning from Galgal, where the idols were, he said to the king: I have a secret message to thee, o king. And he commanded silence: & all being gone forth that were about him,

text.NRSV: Ehud came to him, while he was sitting alone in his cool roof chamber, and said, “I have a message from God for you.” So he rose from his seat.
text.Rotherham: And, Ehud, came in unto him, he, having been sitting in a summer parlour, which he had for himself, alone, and Ehud said, A divine word, have I, unto thee. So he arose from off his seat.
text.KJV: And Ehud came unto him; and he was sitting in a summer parlour, which he had for himself alone. And Ehud said, I have a message from God unto thee. And he arose out of his seat.
text.NABRE: Ehud went in to him where he sat alone in his cool upper room. Ehud said, “I have a word from God for you.” So the king rose from his throne.
text.DRB: Aod went in to him: and he sat in a summer chamber alone, and he said: A word from God I have to thee. Who forthwith rose out of his throne.

text.NRSV: Then Ehud reached with his left hand, took the sword from his right thigh, and thrust it into Eglon’s belly;
text.Rotherham: And Ehud put forth his left hand, and took the sword from off his right thigh,—and thrust it into his body;
text.KJV: And Ehud put forth his left hand, and took the dagger from his right thigh, and thrust it into his belly:
text.NABRE: Then Ehud with his left hand drew the dagger from his right thigh, and thrust it into Eglon’s belly.
text.DRB: And Aod put forth his left hand, & took the dagger from his right thigh, and fastened it into his belly

text.NRSV: the hilt also went in after the blade, and the fat closed over the blade, for he did not draw the sword out of his belly; and the dirt came out.
text.Rotherham: and, the handle also, went in after the blade, and the flesh closed upon the blade, for he withdrew not the sword out of his body,—and he came out into the ante-chamber.
text.KJV: And the haft also went in after the blade; and the fat closed upon the blade, so that he could not draw the dagger out of his belly; and the dirt came out.
text.NABRE: The hilt also went in after the blade, and the fat closed over the blade because he did not withdraw the dagger from the body.
text.DRB: so mightily that the haft followed the blade in the wound, and was closed up fast with the most fat grease. Neither did he pluck out the dagger, but as he had strucken so left it in the body: and forthwith by the secret parts of nature the ordure of the belly came forth.

text.NRSV: Then Ehud went out into the vestibule, and closed the doors of the roof chamber on him, and locked them.
text.Rotherham: And, when Ehud came out into the porch, he closed the doors of the parlour upon him, and bolted them.
text.KJV: Then Ehud went forth through the porch, and shut the doors of the parlour upon him, and locked them.
text.NABRE: Then Ehud went out onto the porch, shutting the doors of the upper room on Eglon and locking them.
text.DRB: But Aod shutting the doors of the chamber very diligently, & locking them sure,

text.NRSV: After he had gone, the servants came. When they saw that the doors of the roof chamber were locked, they thought, “He must be relieving himself in the cool chamber.”
text.Rotherham: When, he, had come out, his servants, went in, and looked, and lo! the doors of the parlour were bolted,—so they said, Surely he covereth his feet, in the summer chamber.
text.KJV: When he was gone out, his servants came; and when they saw that, behold, the doors of the parlour were locked, they said, Surely he covereth his feet in his summer chamber.
text.NABRE: When Ehud had left and the servants had come, they saw that the doors of the upper room were locked, and thought, “He must be easing himself in the cool chamber.”
text.DRB: went out by a postern door. And the king's servants going in, saw the doors of the chamber shut, and they said: Peradventure he purgeth his belly in the summer chamber.

text.NRSV: So they waited until they were embarrassed. When he still did not open the doors of the roof chamber, they took the key and opened them. There was their lord lying dead on the floor.
text.Rotherham: But, though they tarried a long time, yet lo! he opened not the doors of the parlour,—so they took the key, and opened [them], when lo! their lord, fallen to the ground dead.
text.KJV: And they tarried till they were ashamed: and, behold, he opened not the doors of the parlour; therefore they took a key, and opened them: and, behold, their lord was fallen down dead on the earth.
text.NABRE: They waited until they were at a loss when he did not open the doors of the upper room. So they took the key and opened them, and there was their lord lying on the floor, dead.
text.DRB: And expecting long till they were ashamed, & seeing that no man did open, they took a key: and opening they found their Lord on the earth lying dead.

text.NRSV: Ehud escaped while they delayed, and passed beyond the sculptured stones, and escaped to Seirah.
text.Rotherham: But, Ehud, escaped while they delayed,—yea, he, passed the images, and escaped into Seirah.
text.KJV: And Ehud escaped while they tarried, and passed beyond the quarries, and escaped unto Seirath.
text.NABRE: During their delay Ehud escaped and, passing the sculptured stones, took refuge in Seirah.
text.DRB: But Aod, whiles they were troubled, escaped, and passed by the Place of idols, whence he had returned. And he came into Seirath:

text.NRSV: When he arrived, he sounded the trumpet in the hill country of Ephraim; and the Israelites went down with him from the hill country, having him at their head.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, when he came, that he blew with a horn, throughout the hill country of Ephraim,—and the sons of Israel came down with him, out of the hill country, he, being before them.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when he was come, that he blew a trumpet in the mountain of Ephraim, and the children of Israel went down with him from the mount, and he before them.
text.NABRE: On his arrival he sounded the horn in the mountain region of Ephraim, and the Israelites went down from the mountains with him as their leader.
text.DRB: and forthwith the trumpet sounded in the mount of Ephraim: and the children of Israel went down with him, himself going in the front.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Follow after me; for the Lord has given your enemies the Moabites into your hand.” So they went down after him, and seized the fords of the Jordan against the Moabites, and allowed no one to cross over.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Come down after me, for Yahweh hath delivered your enemies, the Moabites, into your hand. So they came down after him, and captured the fords of the Jordan, towards Moab, and suffered not a man to pass over.
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Follow after me: for the Lord hath delivered your enemies the Moabites into your hand. And they went down after him, and took the fords of Jordan toward Moab, and suffered not a man to pass over.
text.NABRE: “Follow me,” he said to them, “for the Lord has delivered your enemies the Moabites into your power.” So they followed him down and seized the fords of the Jordan against the Moabites, permitting no one to cross.
text.DRB: Who said to them: Follow me: for our Lord hath delivered our enemies the Moabites into our hands. And they went down after him, and occupied the fords of Jordan, which bring over into Moab: & they suffered no man to pass:

text.NRSV: At that time they killed about ten thousand of the Moabites, all strong, able-bodied men; no one escaped.
text.Rotherham: And they smote of Moab, at that time, about ten thousand men, every one a mighty man, and every one a man of valour,—and, there escaped not a man.
text.KJV: And they slew of Moab at that time about ten thousand men, all lusty, and all men of valour; and there escaped not a man.
text.NABRE: On that occasion they slew about ten thousand Moabites, all of them strong warriors. Not one escaped.
text.DRB: but they struck the Moabites at that time, about ten thousand, all stout and strong men, none of them could escape.

text.NRSV: So Moab was subdued that day under the hand of Israel. And the land had rest eighty years.
text.Rotherham: And Moab was subdued on that day, under the hand of Israel. And the land had rest eighty years, and Ehud judged them until his death.
text.KJV: So Moab was subdued that day under the hand of Israel. And the land had rest fourscore years.
text.NABRE: So Moab was brought under the power of Israel at that time; and the land had rest for eighty years.
text.DRB: And Moab was humbled that day under the hand of Israel: and the Land rested eighty years.

text.NRSV: After him came Shamgar son of Anath, who killed six hundred of the Philistines with an oxgoad. He too delivered Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, after him, was Shamgar, son of Anath, who smote of the Philistines, six hundred men, with an ox-goad,—and, he also, saved Israel.
text.KJV: And after him was Shamgar the son of Anath, which slew of the Philistines six hundred men with an ox goad: and he also delivered Israel.
text.NABRE: Shamgar. After him there was Shamgar, son of Anath, who slew six hundred Philistines with an oxgoad. He, too, was a savior for Israel.
text.DRB: After him was Samgar the son of Anath, who struck of the Philisthimes six hundred men with the coulter of a plough: and he also defended Israel.

text.NRSV: The Israelites again did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, after Ehud died.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel again did the thing that was wicked in the sight of Yahweh,—when, Ehud, was dead.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel again did evil in the sight of the Lord, when Ehud was dead.
text.NABRE: The Israelites again did what was evil in the sight of the Lord; Ehud was dead.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel added to do evil in the sight of our Lord after the death of Aod,

text.NRSV: So the Lord sold them into the hand of King Jabin of Canaan, who reigned in Hazor; the commander of his army was Sisera, who lived in Harosheth-ha-goiim.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh sold them into the hand of Jabin king of Canaan, who reigned in Hazor,—now, the prince of his host, was Sisera, and, he, dwelt in Harosheth of the nations.
text.KJV: And the Lord sold them into the hand of Jabin king of Canaan, that reigned in Hazor; the captain of whose host was Sisera, which dwelt in Harosheth of the Gentiles.
text.NABRE: So the Lord sold them into the power of the Canaanite king, Jabin, who reigned in Hazor. The general of his army was Sisera, who lived in Harosheth-ha-goiim.
text.DRB: and our Lord delivered them into the hands of Jabin the king of Chanaan, which reigned in Asor: and he had a captain of his army named Sisara, and he dwelt in Haroseth of the gentiles.

text.NRSV: Then the Israelites cried out to the Lord for help; for he had nine hundred chariots of iron, and had oppressed the Israelites cruelly twenty years.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel made outcry unto Yahweh,—for he had, nine hundred chariots of iron, and, he, oppressed the sons of Israel heavily, twenty years.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel cried unto the Lord: for he had nine hundred chariots of iron; and twenty years he mightily oppressed the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: But the Israelites cried out to the Lord; for with his nine hundred iron chariots Jabin harshly oppressed the Israelites for twenty years.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel cried to our Lord, for he had nine hundred iron hooked chariots, and for twenty years had vehemently oppressed them.

text.NRSV: At that time Deborah, a prophetess, wife of Lappidoth, was judging Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, Deborah, a woman who was a prophetess, wife of Lapidoth, she, was judging Israel, at that time:
text.KJV: And Deborah, a prophetess, the wife of Lapidoth, she judged Israel at that time.
text.NABRE: At that time the prophet Deborah, wife of Lappidoth, was judging Israel.
text.DRB: And there was one Debbora a prophetess, the wife of Lapidoth, which judged the people at that time.

text.NRSV: She used to sit under the palm of Deborah between Ramah and Bethel in the hill country of Ephraim; and the Israelites came up to her for judgment.
text.Rotherham: she used to sit, therefore, under the palm-tree of Deborah, between Ramah and Bethel, in the hill country of Ephraim,—and the sons of Israel came up to her, for justice.
text.KJV: And she dwelt under the palm tree of Deborah between Ramah and Beth–el in mount Ephraim: and the children of Israel came up to her for judgment.
text.NABRE: She used to sit under Deborah’s palm tree, between Ramah and Bethel in the mountain region of Ephraim, where the Israelites came up to her for judgment.
text.DRB: And she sat under a palm tree, which was called by her name, between Rama and Bethel in the mount of Ephraim: and the children of Israel went up to her for all judgment.

text.NRSV: She sent and summoned Barak son of Abinoam from Kedesh in Naphtali, and said to him, “The Lord, the God of Israel, commands you, ‘Go, take position at Mount Tabor, bringing ten thousand from the tribe of Naphtali and the tribe of Zebulun.
text.Rotherham: And she sent and called for Barak son of Abinoam, out of Kadesh-naphtali,—and said unto him—Hath not Yahweh God of Israel, commanded,—Come and draw towards Mount Tabor, and bring with thee—ten thousand men, of the sons of Naphtali, and of the sons of Zebulun;
text.KJV: And she sent and called Barak the son of Abinoam out of Kedesh–naphtali, and said unto him, Hath not the Lord God of Israel commanded, saying, Go and draw toward mount Tabor, and take with thee ten thousand men of the children of Naphtali and of the children of Zebulun?
text.NABRE: She had Barak, son of Abinoam, summoned from Kedesh of Naphtali. She said to him, “This is what the Lord, the God of Israel, commands: Go, march against Mount Tabor, and take with you ten thousand men from Naphtali and Zebulun.
text.DRB: Who sent, and called Barac the son of Abinoem of Cedes in Nephthali, and she said to him: Our Lord God of Israel hath commanded thee: Go, and lead an army into mount Thabor, and thou shalt take with thee ten thousand fighting men of the children of Nephthali, & of the children of Zabulon:

text.NRSV: I will draw out Sisera, the general of Jabin’s army, to meet you by the Wadi Kishon with his chariots and his troops; and I will give him into your hand.’”
text.Rotherham: and I will draw unto thee, unto the torrent of Kishon, Sisera, prince of the host of Jabin, with his chariots, and with his multitude,—and will deliver him into thy hand?
text.KJV: And I will draw unto thee to the river Kishon Sisera, the captain of Jabin’s army, with his chariots and his multitude; and I will deliver him into thine hand.
text.NABRE: I will draw Sisera, the general of Jabin’s army, out to you at the Wadi Kishon, together with his chariots and troops, and I will deliver them into your power.”
text.DRB: and I will bring unto thee in the place of the Torrent Cison, Sisara the Prince of the host of Jabin, and his chariots, and all the multitude, and will deliver them in thy hand.

text.NRSV: Barak said to her, “If you will go with me, I will go; but if you will not go with me, I will not go.”
text.Rotherham: And Barak said unto her, If thou wilt go with me, then will I go—but, if thou wilt not go with me, I will not go.
text.KJV: And Barak said unto her, If thou wilt go with me, then I will go: but if thou wilt not go with me, then I will not go.
text.NABRE: But Barak answered her, “If you come with me, I will go; if you do not come with me, I will not go.”
text.DRB: And Barac said to her: If thou come with me, I will go: If thou wilt not come with me, I will not go.

text.NRSV: And she said, “I will surely go with you; nevertheless, the road on which you are going will not lead to your glory, for the Lord will sell Sisera into the hand of a woman.” Then Deborah got up and went with Barak to Kedesh.
text.Rotherham: And she said—I will, go, with thee; only, it shall not be, thine own honour, that shall arise from the journey which thou art about to take, for, into the hand of a woman, will Yahweh sell Sisera. So Deborah arose, and went with Barak, towards Kadesh.
text.KJV: And she said, I will surely go with thee: notwithstanding the journey that thou takest shall not be for thine honour; for the Lord shall sell Sisera into the hand of a woman. And Deborah arose, and went with Barak to Kedesh.
text.NABRE: “I will certainly go with you,” she replied, “but you will not gain glory for the expedition on which you are setting out, for it is into a woman’s power that the Lord is going to sell Sisera.” So Deborah arose and went with Barak and journeyed with him to Kedesh.
text.DRB: Who said to him: I will go indeed with thee, but at this time the victory shall not be imputed to thee, because Sisara shall be delivered into the hand of a woman. Debbora therefore arose, and went with Barac into Cedes.

text.NRSV: Barak summoned Zebulun and Naphtali to Kedesh; and ten thousand warriors went up behind him; and Deborah went up with him.
text.Rotherham: And Barak called together Zebulun and Naphtali, towards Kadesh, and there went up at his feet—ten thousand men,—and Deborah, went up with him.
text.KJV: And Barak called Zebulun and Naphtali to Kedesh; and he went up with ten thousand men at his feet: and Deborah went up with him.
text.NABRE: Barak summoned Zebulun and Naphtali to Kedesh, and ten thousand men followed him. Deborah also went up with him.
text.DRB: Who calling unto him Zabulon and Nephthali, went up with ten thousand fighting men, having Debbora in his company.

text.NRSV: Now Heber the Kenite had separated from the other Kenites, that is, the descendants of Hobab the father-in-law of Moses, and had encamped as far away as Elon-bezaanannim, which is near Kedesh.
text.Rotherham: Now, Heber the Kenite, had separated himself from the Kenites, even from the sons of Hobab, father-in-law of Moses,—and moved his tent as far as the oak of Zaanaim, which is near Kadesh.
text.KJV: Now Heber the Kenite, which was of the children of Hobab the father in law of Moses, had severed himself from the Kenites, and pitched his tent unto the plain of Zaanaim, which is by Kedesh.
text.NABRE: Now Heber the Kenite had detached himself from Cain, the descendants of Hobab, Moses’ father-in-law, and had pitched his tent by the terebinth of Zaanannim, which was near Kedesh.
text.DRB: And Haber the Cineite was in time past departed from the rest of the Cineites his brethren of Hobab, the cousin of Moyses: and had pitched his tents unto the valley, which is called Sennim, and was near Cedes.

text.NRSV: When Sisera was told that Barak son of Abinoam had gone up to Mount Tabor,
text.Rotherham: And they told Sisera that Barak, son of Abinoam, had gone up to Mount Tabor.
