Who compassing the fort burnt it: and so it came to pass, that with the smoke and the fire a thousand persons were slain, men and women together, of the inhabitants of the tower of Sichem.

text.NRSV: Then Abimelech went to Thebez, and encamped against Thebez, and took it.
text.Rotherham: Then went Abimelech unto Thebez,—and encamped against Thebez, and captured it.
text.KJV: Then went Abimelech to Thebez, and encamped against Thebez, and took it.
text.NABRE: Abimelech proceeded to Thebez, encamped, and captured it.
text.DRB: And Abimelech departing thence came to the town of Thebes, which compassing he besieged with his army.

text.NRSV: But there was a strong tower within the city, and all the men and women and all the lords of the city fled to it and shut themselves in; and they went to the roof of the tower.
text.Rotherham: Now, a strong tower, was in the midst of the city, and there fled thither all the men and the women, and all the owners of the city, and shut themselves in,—and went up on the roof of the tower.
text.KJV: But there was a strong tower within the city, and thither fled all the men and women, and all they of the city, and shut it to them, and gat them up to the top of the tower.
text.NABRE: Now there was a strong tower in the middle of the city, and all the men and women and all the lords of the city fled there, shutting themselves in and going up to the roof of the tower.
text.DRB: And there was in the midst of the city an high tower, to the which were fled both men and women together, and all the Princes of the city, the gate being shut very strongly, and they standing upon the battlements of the tower by the bulwarks.

text.NRSV: Abimelech came to the tower, and fought against it, and came near to the entrance of the tower to burn it with fire.
text.Rotherham: And Abimelech came in as far as the tower, and fought against it,—and so came near, as far as the entrance of the tower, to burn it with fire.
text.KJV: And Abimelech came unto the tower, and fought against it, and went hard unto the door of the tower to burn it with fire.
text.NABRE: Abimelech came up to the tower and fought against it. When he came close to the entrance of the tower to set it on fire,
text.DRB: And Abimelech coming near the tower, fought manfully: & approaching to the door, endeavoured to put fire under it:

text.NRSV: But a certain woman threw an upper millstone on Abimelech’s head, and crushed his skull.
text.Rotherham: And a certain woman cast an upper millstone upon the head of Abimelech,—and brake in pieces his skull.
text.KJV: And a certain woman cast a piece of a millstone upon Abimelech’s head, and all to brake his skull.
text.NABRE: a certain woman cast the upper part of a millstone down on Abimelech’s head, and it fractured his skull.
text.DRB: and behold one woman casting from above a piece of a millstone, dashed it against the head of Abimelech, and brake his brain.

text.NRSV: Immediately he called to the young man who carried his armor and said to him, “Draw your sword and kill me, so people will not say about me, ‘A woman killed him.’” So the young man thrust him through, and he died.
text.Rotherham: Then called he hastily unto the young man bearing his armour, and said unto him—Draw thy sword, and put me to death, lest they say of me, A woman, slew him! So his young man thrust him through, and he died.
text.KJV: Then he called hastily unto the young man his armourbearer, and said unto him, Draw thy sword, and slay me, that men say not of me, A woman slew him. And his young man thrust him through, and he died.
text.NABRE: He immediately called his armor-bearer and said to him, “Draw your sword and put me to death so they will not say about me, ‘A woman killed him.’” So his attendant ran him through and he died.
text.DRB: Who called by and by his esquire, and said to him: Draw out thy sword, and strike me: lest perhaps it be said that I was slain of a woman. Who doing as he was commanded slew him.

text.NRSV: When the Israelites saw that Abimelech was dead, they all went home.
text.Rotherham: And, when the men of Israel saw that Abimelech was, dead, they went their way every man to his own place.
text.KJV: And when the men of Israel saw that Abimelech was dead, they departed every man unto his place.
text.NABRE: When the Israelites saw that Abimelech was dead, they all left for their homes.
text.DRB: And when he was dead, all that were with him of Israel returned into their seats:

text.NRSV: Thus God repaid Abimelech for the crime he committed against his father in killing his seventy brothers;
text.Rotherham: Thus did God requite the wickedness of Abimelech, which he had done to his father, in slaying his seventy brethren;
text.KJV: Thus God rendered the wickedness of Abimelech, which he did unto his father, in slaying his seventy brethren:
text.NABRE: Thus did God repay the evil that Abimelech had done to his father in killing his seventy brothers.
text.DRB: and God repayed the evil, that Abimelech had done against his father, killing his seventy brethren.

text.NRSV: and God also made all the wickedness of the people of Shechem fall back on their heads, and on them came the curse of Jotham son of Jerubbaal.
text.Rotherham: all the wickedness also, of the men of Shechem, did God requite upon their own head,—and so brought in upon, them the curse of Jotham, son of Jerubbaal.
text.KJV: And all the evil of the men of Shechem did God render upon their heads: and upon them came the curse of Jotham the son of Jerubbaal.
text.NABRE: God also brought all the wickedness of the people of Shechem back on their heads, for the curse of Jotham, son of Jerubbaal, overtook them.
text.DRB: The Sichemites were also rewarded for that which they had wrought, and the curse of Joatham the son of Jerobaal came upon them.

text.NRSV: After Abimelech, Tola son of Puah son of Dodo, a man of Issachar, who lived at Shamir in the hill country of Ephraim, rose to deliver Israel.
text.Rotherham: And there arose, after Abimelech, to save Israel—Tola son of Puah son of Dodo, a man of Issachar,—and, he, used to sit in Shamir, in the hill country of Ephraim;
text.KJV: And after Abimelech there arose to defend Israel Tola the son of Puah, the son of Dodo, a man of Issachar; and he dwelt in Shamir in mount Ephraim.
text.NABRE: After Abimelech, Tola, son of Puah, son of Dodo, a man of Issachar, rose up to save Israel; he lived in Shamir in the mountain region of Ephraim.
text.DRB: After Abimelech there arose Ruler in Israel Thola the son of Phua the uncle of Abimelech, a man of Issachar, which dwelt in Samir of mount Ephraim:

text.NRSV: He judged Israel twenty-three years. Then he died, and was buried at Shamir.
text.Rotherham: and he judged Israel, twenty-three years,—and died, and was buried in Shamir.
text.KJV: And he judged Israel twenty and three years, and died, and was buried in Shamir.
text.NABRE: When he had judged Israel twenty-three years, he died and was buried in Shamir.
text.DRB: and judged Israel three and twenty years, and died and was buried in Samir.

text.NRSV: After him came Jair the Gileadite, who judged Israel twenty-two years.
text.Rotherham: And there arose, after him, Jair the Gileadite,—and judged Israel twenty-two years.
text.KJV: And after him arose Jair, a Gileadite, and judged Israel twenty and two years.
text.NABRE: Jair. Jair the Gileadite came after him and judged Israel twenty-two years.
text.DRB: After him succeeded Jair the Galaadite, who judged Israel for two and twenty years,

text.NRSV: He had thirty sons who rode on thirty donkeys; and they had thirty towns, which are in the land of Gilead, and are called Havvoth-jair to this day.
text.Rotherham: Now he had thirty sons, that rode on thirty ass colts, and they had, thirty cities,—they are called Havvoth-jair, until this day, which are in the land of Gilead.
text.KJV: And he had thirty sons that rode on thirty ass colts, and they had thirty cities, which are called Havoth–jair unto this day, which are in the land of Gilead.
text.NABRE: He had thirty sons who rode on thirty donkeys and possessed thirty cities in the land of Gilead (these are called Havvoth-jair to the present day).
text.DRB: having thirty sons sitting upon thirty ass colts, & Princes of thirty cities, which of his name were called Havoth Jair, that is, the towns of Jair, until this present day, in the Land of Galaad.

text.NRSV: Jair died, and was buried in Kamon.
text.Rotherham: And Jair died, and was buried in Kamon.
text.KJV: And Jair died, and was buried in Camon.
text.NABRE: Jair died and was buried in Kamon.
text.DRB: And Jair died, and was buried in the place, which is called Camon.

text.NRSV: The Israelites again did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, worshiping the Baals and the Astartes, the gods of Aram, the gods of Sidon, the gods of Moab, the gods of the Ammonites, and the gods of the Philistines. Thus they abandoned the Lord, and did not worship him.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel again did the thing that was wicked in the sight of Yahweh, and served the Baals and the Ashtoreths, and the gods of Syria, and the gods of Zidon, and the gods of Moab, and the gods of the sons of Ammon, and the gods of the Philistines,—and forsook Yahweh, and served him not.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel did evil again in the sight of the Lord, and served Baalim, and Ashtaroth, and the gods of Syria, and the gods of Zidon, and the gods of Moab, and the gods of the children of Ammon, and the gods of the Philistines, and forsook the Lord, and served not him.
text.NABRE: Oppression by the Ammonites. The Israelites again did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, serving the Baals and Ashtarts, the gods of Aram, the gods of Sidon, the gods of Moab, the gods of the Ammonites, and the gods of the Philistines. Since they had abandoned the Lord and would not serve him,
text.DRB: But the children of Israel joining new sins to their old, did evil in the sight of our Lord, & served the idols, Baalim and Astaroth, & the gods of Syria and of Sidon and of Moab and of the children of Ammon and of the Philisthiims: and they left our Lord, and did not serve him.

text.NRSV: So the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel, and he sold them into the hand of the Philistines and into the hand of the Ammonites,
text.Rotherham: So the anger of Yahweh kindled upon Israel,—and he sold them into the hand of the Philistines, and into the hand of the sons of Ammon;
text.KJV: And the anger of the Lord was hot against Israel, and he sold them into the hands of the Philistines, and into the hands of the children of Ammon.
text.NABRE: the Lord became angry with Israel and he sold them into the power of the Philistines and the Ammonites.
text.DRB: Against whom our Lord being wrath, delivered them into the hands of the Philisthiims and of the children of Ammon.

text.NRSV: and they crushed and oppressed the Israelites that year. For eighteen years they oppressed all the Israelites that were beyond the Jordan in the land of the Amorites, which is in Gilead.
text.Rotherham: and they enfeebled and oppressed the sons of Israel in that year,—eighteen years, did they this unto all the sons of Israel who were beyond the Jordan, in the land of the Amorites, that was in Gilead.
text.KJV: And that year they vexed and oppressed the children of Israel: eighteen years, all the children of Israel that were on the other side Jordan in the land of the Amorites, which is in Gilead.
text.NABRE: For eighteen years they afflicted and oppressed the Israelites in Bashan, and all the Israelites in the Amorite land beyond the Jordan in Gilead.
text.DRB: And they were afflicted and sore oppressed for eighteen years, all that dwelt beyond Jordan in the Land of the Amorrhite, which is in Galaad:

text.NRSV: The Ammonites also crossed the Jordan to fight against Judah and against Benjamin and against the house of Ephraim; so that Israel was greatly distressed.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Ammon crossed the Jordan, to fight, even against Judah and against Benjamin, and against the house of Ephraim,—so that Israel was sore distressed.
text.KJV: Moreover the children of Ammon passed over Jordan to fight also against Judah, and against Benjamin, and against the house of Ephraim; so that Israel was sore distressed.
text.NABRE: The Ammonites also crossed the Jordan to fight against Judah, Benjamin and the house of Ephraim, so that Israel was in great distress.
text.DRB: in so much, that the children of Ammon passing over Jordan wasted Judas and Benjamin and Ephraim: and Israel was afflicted exceedingly.

text.NRSV: So the Israelites cried to the Lord, saying, “We have sinned against you, because we have abandoned our God and have worshiped the Baals.”
text.Rotherham: Then did the sons of Israel make outcry unto Yahweh, saying,—We have sinned against thee, because we have forsaken our God, and have served the Baals.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel cried unto the Lord, saying, We have sinned against thee, both because we have forsaken our God, and also served Baalim.
text.NABRE: Then the Israelites cried out to the Lord, “We have sinned against you, for we have abandoned our God and served the Baals.”
text.DRB: And crying to our Lord, they said: We have sinned to thee, because we have forsaken our Lord God & have served Baalim.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said to the Israelites, “Did I not deliver you from the Egyptians and from the Amorites, from the Ammonites and from the Philistines?
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto the sons of Israel,—Was it not, from the Egyptians, and from the Amorites, and from the sons of Ammon, and from the Philistines, [that I saved you]?
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto the children of Israel, Did not I deliver you from the Egyptians, and from the Amorites, from the children of Ammon, and from the Philistines?
text.NABRE: The Lord answered the Israelites: Did not the Egyptians, the Amorites, the Ammonites, the Philistines,
text.DRB: To whom our Lord spake: Have not the Ægyptians and the Amorrheites, and the children of Ammon and the Philisthiims,

text.NRSV: The Sidonians also, and the Amalekites, and the Maonites, oppressed you; and you cried to me, and I delivered you out of their hand.
text.Rotherham: The Zidonians also, and the Amalekites, and the Maonites, oppressed you,—and ye made outcry unto me, and I saved you out of their hand.
text.KJV: The Zidonians also, and the Amalekites, and the Maonites, did oppress you; and ye cried to me, and I delivered you out of their hand.
text.NABRE: the Sidonians, the Amalekites, and the Midianites oppress you? Yet when you cried out to me, and I saved you from their power,
text.DRB: the Sidonians also and Amalech and Chanaan oppressed you, & you cried to me, and I delivered you out of their hand?

text.NRSV: Yet you have abandoned me and worshiped other gods; therefore I will deliver you no more.
text.Rotherham: Yet have, ye, forsaken me, and served other gods,—therefore will I not again save you.
text.KJV: Yet ye have forsaken me, and served other gods: wherefore I will deliver you no more.
text.NABRE: you still abandoned me and served other gods. Therefore I will save you no more.
text.DRB: And yet you have forsaken me, and have worshipped strange gods: therefore I will not add to deliver you any more:

text.NRSV: Go and cry to the gods whom you have chosen; let them deliver you in the time of your distress.”
text.Rotherham: Go and make outcry unto the gods whom ye have chosen,—they, must save you, in the time of your tribulation.
text.KJV: Go and cry unto the gods which ye have chosen; let them deliver you in the time of your tribulation.
text.NABRE: Go and cry out to the gods you have chosen; let them save you in your time of distress.
text.DRB: go and invocate the gods which you have chosen: let them deliver you in the time of distress.

text.NRSV: And the Israelites said to the Lord, “We have sinned; do to us whatever seems good to you; but deliver us this day!”
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel said unto Yahweh—We have sinned, do, thou, with us, according to all that is fitting in thine eyes,—only rescue us, we beseech thee this day.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel said unto the Lord, We have sinned: do thou unto us whatsoever seemeth good unto thee; deliver us only, we pray thee, this day.
text.NABRE: But the Israelites said to the Lord, “We have sinned. Do to us whatever is good in your sight. Only deliver us this day!”
text.DRB: And the children of Israel said to our Lord: We have sinned, render to us whatsoever pleaseth thee: only now deliver us.

text.NRSV: So they put away the foreign gods from among them and worshiped the Lord; and he could no longer bear to see Israel suffer.
text.Rotherham: And they put away the gods of the stranger out of their midst, and served Yahweh,—and his soul was impatient of the misery of Israel.
text.KJV: And they put away the strange gods from among them, and served the Lord: and his soul was grieved for the misery of Israel.
text.NABRE: And they cast out the foreign gods from their midst and served the Lord, so that he grieved over the misery of Israel.
text.DRB: In saying which things, they threw away out of their coasts all the idols of strange gods, and served our Lord God: who sorrowed for their miseries.

text.NRSV: Then the Ammonites were called to arms, and they encamped in Gilead; and the Israelites came together, and they encamped at Mizpah.
text.Rotherham: Now the sons of Ammon were called out, and they encamped in Gilead,—and the sons of Israel assembled themselves together, and encamped in Mizpah.
text.KJV: Then the children of Ammon were gathered together, and encamped in Gilead. And the children of Israel assembled themselves together, and encamped in Mizpeh.
text.NABRE: The Ammonites were called out for war and encamped in Gilead, while the Israelites assembled and encamped at Mizpah.
text.DRB: Therefore the children of Ammon crying together pitcht their tents in Galaad: against whom the children of Israel being assembled camped in Maspha.

text.NRSV: The commanders of the people of Gilead said to one another, “Who will begin the fight against the Ammonites? He shall be head over all the inhabitants of Gilead.”
text.Rotherham: Then said the people, the princes of Gilead, one to another, Who is the man that will begin to fight against the sons of Ammon? he shall become head to all the inhabitants of Gilead.
text.KJV: And the people and princes of Gilead said one to another, What man is he that will begin to fight against the children of Ammon? he shall be head over all the inhabitants of Gilead.
text.NABRE: The captains of the army of Gilead said to one another, “The one who begins the war against the Ammonites shall be leader of all the inhabitants of Gilead.”
text.DRB: And the Princes of Galaad said every one to their neighbours: Who of us shall first begin to fight against the children of Ammon, shall be the Duke of the people of Galaad.

text.NRSV: Now Jephthah the Gileadite, the son of a prostitute, was a mighty warrior. Gilead was the father of Jephthah.
text.Rotherham: Now, Jephthah the Gileadite, was a mighty man of valour, but, he, was the son of an unchaste woman,—yet Gilead was the father of Jephthah.
text.KJV: Now Jephthah the Gileadite was a mighty man of valour, and he was the son of an harlot: and Gilead begat Jephthah.
text.NABRE: Jephthah the Gileadite was a warrior. He was the son of a prostitute, fathered by Gilead.
text.DRB: There was at that time Jephte the Galaadite, a most valiant man & a warrior, the son of a woman that was an harlot, who was born of Galaad.

text.NRSV: Gilead’s wife also bore him sons; and when his wife’s sons grew up, they drove Jephthah away, saying to him, “You shall not inherit anything in our father’s house; for you are the son of another woman.”
text.Rotherham: And the wife of Gilead bare him sons,—and, when the wife’s sons grew up, they thrust out Jephthah, and said unto him—Thou shalt not inherit with the house of our father, for, son of an alien woman, art thou.
text.KJV: And Gilead’s wife bare him sons; and his wife’s sons grew up, and they thrust out Jephthah, and said unto him, Thou shalt not inherit in our father’s house; for thou art the son of a strange woman.
text.NABRE: Gilead’s wife had also borne him sons. When they grew up the sons of the wife had driven Jephthah away, saying to him, “You shall inherit nothing in our father’s house, for you are the son of another woman.”
text.DRB: And Galaad had a wife of whom he had sons: who after they were grown, cast out Jephte, saying: Thou canst not be heir in the house of our father, because thou art born of another mother.

text.NRSV: Then Jephthah fled from his brothers and lived in the land of Tob. Outlaws collected around Jephthah and went raiding with him.
text.Rotherham: So Jephthah fled from the face of his brethren, and dwelt in the land of Tob,—and there gathered about Jephthah unemployed men, who went forth with him.
text.KJV: Then Jephthah fled from his brethren, and dwelt in the land of Tob: and there were gathered vain men to Jephthah, and went out with him.
text.NABRE: So Jephthah had fled from his brothers and taken up residence in the land of Tob. Worthless men had joined company with him, and went out with him on raids.
text.DRB: Whom he flying & avoiding, dwelt in the Land of Tob: and there were gathered to him needy men, & thievish, & followed him as their Prince.

text.NRSV: After a time the Ammonites made war against Israel.
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass, after a time,—that the sons of Ammon made war with Israel.
text.KJV: And it came to pass in process of time, that the children of Ammon made war against Israel.
text.NABRE: Some time later, the Ammonites went to war with Israel.
text.DRB: In those days the children of Ammon fought against Israel:

text.NRSV: And when the Ammonites made war against Israel, the elders of Gilead went to bring Jephthah from the land of Tob.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, it came to pass that the sons of Ammon made war with Israel, then went the elders of Gilead, to summon Jephthah out of the land of Tob.
text.KJV: And it was so, that when the children of Ammon made war against Israel, the elders of Gilead went to fetch Jephthah out of the land of Tob:
text.NABRE: As soon as the Ammonites were at war with Israel, the elders of Gilead went to bring Jephthah from the land of Tob.
text.DRB: who pressing sore upon them, the ancients of Galaad went to take Jephte out of the Land of Tob to help them:

text.NRSV: They said to Jephthah, “Come and be our commander, so that we may fight with the Ammonites.”
text.Rotherham: And they said to Jephthah, Oh! come and be our commander,—that we may do battle with the sons of Ammon.
text.KJV: And they said unto Jephthah, Come, and be our captain, that we may fight with the children of Ammon.
text.NABRE: “Come,” they said to Jephthah, “be our commander so that we can fight the Ammonites.”
text.DRB: & they said to him: Come & be our Prince, & fight against the children of Ammon.

text.NRSV: But Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, “Are you not the very ones who rejected me and drove me out of my father’s house? So why do you come to me now when you are in trouble?”
text.Rotherham: But Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, Have not, ye, hated me, and thrust me out from the house of my father? Wherefore then, are ye come unto me, now, when ye are in distress?
text.KJV: And Jephthah said unto the elders of Gilead, Did not ye hate me, and expel me out of my father’s house? and why are ye come unto me now when ye are in distress?
text.NABRE: “Are you not the ones who hated me and drove me from my father’s house?” Jephthah replied to the elders of Gilead, “Why do you come to me now, when you are in distress?”
text.DRB: To whom he answered: Are not you they that hated me, & cast me out of my father's house, and now are come to me forced by necessity.

text.NRSV: The elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, “Nevertheless, we have now turned back to you, so that you may go with us and fight with the Ammonites, and become head over us, over all the inhabitants of Gilead.”
text.Rotherham: And the elders of Gilead said unto Jephthah—Therefore, have we, now, returned unto thee, that, if thou go with us, and do battle with the sons of Ammon, then shalt thou become our head, for all the inhabitants of Gilead.
text.KJV: And the elders of Gilead said unto Jephthah, Therefore we turn again to thee now, that thou mayest go with us, and fight against the children of Ammon, and be our head over all the inhabitants of Gilead.
text.NABRE: The elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, “This is the reason we have come back to you now: if you go with us to fight against the Ammonites, you shall be the leader of all of the inhabitants of Gilead.”
text.DRB: And the Princes of Galaad said to Jephte: For this cause be we now come to thee, that thou go forth with us, & fight against the children of Ammon, and be the Captain of all that dwell in Galaad.

text.NRSV: Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, “If you bring me home again to fight with the Ammonites, and the Lord gives them over to me, I will be your head.”
text.Rotherham: And Jephthah said unto the elders of Gilead—If ye do bring me back to do battle with the sons of Ammon, and Yahweh deliver them up before me, shall, I myself, become your head?
text.KJV: And Jephthah said unto the elders of Gilead, If ye bring me home again to fight against the children of Ammon, and the Lord deliver them before me, shall I be your head?
text.NABRE: Jephthah answered the elders of Gilead, “If you bring me back to fight against the Ammonites and the Lord delivers them up to me, I will be your leader.”
text.DRB: Jephte also said to them: If you be come to me sincerely, that I should fight for you against the children of Ammon, and if our Lord deliver them into my hands, shall I be your Prince?

text.NRSV: And the elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, “The Lord will be witness between us; we will surely do as you say.”
text.Rotherham: And the elders of Gilead said unto Jephthah—Yahweh himself, will be hearkening between us, if, according to thy word, so, we do not.
text.KJV: And the elders of Gilead said unto Jephthah, The Lord be witness between us, if we do not so according to thy words.
text.NABRE: The elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, “The Lord is witness between us that we will do as you say.”
text.DRB: Who answered him: Our Lord which heareth these things, himself is mediator and witness that we will do as we have promised.

text.NRSV: So Jephthah went with the elders of Gilead, and the people made him head and commander over them; and Jephthah spoke all his words before the Lord at Mizpah.
text.Rotherham: Then went Jephthah with the elders of Gilead, and the people set him over them, as head and commander,—and Jephthah spake all his words before Yahweh, in Mizpah.
text.KJV: Then Jephthah went with the elders of Gilead, and the people made him head and captain over them: and Jephthah uttered all his words before the Lord in Mizpeh.
text.NABRE: So Jephthah went with the elders of Gilead, and the army made him their leader and commander. Jephthah gave all his orders in the presence of the Lord in Mizpah.
text.DRB: Jephte therefore went with the Princes of Galaad, and all the people made him their Prince. And Jephte spake all his words before our Lord in Maspha.

text.NRSV: Then Jephthah sent messengers to the king of the Ammonites and said, “What is there between you and me, that you have come to me to fight against my land?”
text.Rotherham: So then Jephthah sent messengers unto the king of the sons of Ammon, saying,—What occasion is there between us, that thou shouldst have come unto me, to fight against my land?
text.KJV: And Jephthah sent messengers unto the king of the children of Ammon, saying, What hast thou to do with me, that thou art come against me to fight in my land?
text.NABRE: Then he sent messengers to the king of the Ammonites to say, “What do you have against me that you come to fight with me in my land?”
text.DRB: And he sent messengers to the king of the children of Ammon, which should say in his person: What is between me and thee, that thou art come against me, to waste my Land?

text.NRSV: The king of the Ammonites answered the messengers of Jephthah, “Because Israel, on coming from Egypt, took away my land from the Arnon to the Jabbok and to the Jordan; now therefore restore it peaceably.”
text.Rotherham: And the king of the sons of Ammon said unto the messengers of Jephthah—Because Israel took away my land, when they came up out of Egypt, from Arnon even unto the Jabbok, and unto the Jordan,—now, therefore, restore them in peace.
text.KJV: And the king of the children of Ammon answered unto the messengers of Jephthah, Because Israel took away my land, when they came up out of Egypt, from Arnon even unto Jabbok, and unto Jordan: now therefore restore those lands again peaceably.
text.NABRE: The king of the Ammonites answered the messengers of Jephthah, “Israel took away my land from the Arnon to the Jabbok and the Jordan when they came up from Egypt. Now restore it peaceably.”
text.DRB: To whom he answered: Because Israel took my land when he ascended out of Ægypt, from the coasts of Arnon unto Iaboc and Jordan: now therefore with peace restore the same to me.

text.NRSV: Once again Jephthah sent messengers to the king of the Ammonites
text.Rotherham: And Jephthah yet once more sent messengers unto the king of the sons of Ammon;
text.KJV: And Jephthah sent messengers again unto the king of the children of Ammon:
text.NABRE: Again Jephthah sent messengers to the king of the Ammonites,
text.DRB: By whom Jephte again sent word, and commanded them that they should say to the king of Ammon:

text.NRSV: and said to him: “Thus says Jephthah: Israel did not take away the land of Moab or the land of the Ammonites,
text.Rotherham: and said unto him, Thus, saith Jephthah,—Israel took not away the land of Moab, or the land of the sons of Ammon;
text.KJV: And said unto him, Thus saith Jephthah, Israel took not away the land of Moab, nor the land of the children of Ammon:
text.NABRE: saying to him, “This is what Jephthah says: ‘Israel did not take the land of Moab or the land of the Ammonites.
text.DRB: Thus saith Jephte: Israel did not take the Land of Moab, nor the Land of the children of Ammon:

text.NRSV: but when they came up from Egypt, Israel went through the wilderness to the Red Sea and came to Kadesh.
text.Rotherham: for, when they came up out of Egypt, and Israel journeyed through the desert as far as the Red Sea, and came in unto Kadesh,
text.KJV: But when Israel came up from Egypt, and walked through the wilderness unto the Red sea, and came to Kadesh;
text.NABRE: For when they came up from Egypt, Israel went through the wilderness to the Red Sea and came to Kadesh.
text.DRB: but when they ascended out of Ægypt, he walked through the desert unto the Red sea, and came into Cades.

text.NRSV: Israel then sent messengers to the king of Edom, saying, ‘Let us pass through your land’; but the king of Edom would not listen. They also sent to the king of Moab, but he would not consent. So Israel remained at Kadesh.
text.Rotherham: then did Israel send messengers unto the king of Edom, saying—Let me pass, I pray thee, through thy land, but the king of Edom hearkened not, unto the king of Moab also, sent they, but he would not consent,—so Israel abode in Kadesh.
text.KJV: Then Israel sent messengers unto the king of Edom, saying, Let me, I pray thee, pass through thy land: but the king of Edom would not hearken thereto. And in like manner they sent unto the king of Moab: but he would not consent: and Israel abode in Kadesh.
text.NABRE: Israel then sent messengers to the king of Edom saying, “Let me pass through your land.” But the king of Edom did not give consent. They also sent to the king of Moab, but he too was unwilling. So Israel remained in Kadesh.
text.DRB: And he sent messengers to the king of Edom, saying: Suffer me that I may pass through the land. Who would not condescend to his requests. He sent also to the king of Moab, who also himself contemned to give passage. He abode therefore in Cades,

text.NRSV: Then they journeyed through the wilderness, went around the land of Edom and the land of Moab, arrived on the east side of the land of Moab, and camped on the other side of the Arnon. They did not enter the territory of Moab, for the Arnon is the boundary of Moab.
text.Rotherham: Then journeyed they through the desert, and went round the land of Edom, and the land of Moab, and so came, from the rising of the sun, to the land of Moab, but encamped beyond Arnon,—and entered not within the boundary of Moab, for, Arnon, is the boundary of Moab.
text.KJV: Then they went along through the wilderness, and compassed the land of Edom, and the land of Moab, and came by the east side of the land of Moab, and pitched on the other side of Arnon, but came not within the border of Moab: for Arnon was the border of Moab.
text.NABRE: Then they went through the wilderness, and bypassing the land of Edom and the land of Moab, they arrived east of the land of Moab and encamped across the Arnon. Thus they did not enter the territory of Moab, for the Arnon is the boundary of Moab.
text.DRB: and compassed the Land of Edom at the side, and the land of Moab: & came against the East quarter of the Land of Moab, & camped beyond Arnon: neither would he enter the bounds of Moab: for Arnon is the border of the Land of Moab.

text.NRSV: Israel then sent messengers to King Sihon of the Amorites, king of Heshbon; and Israel said to him, ‘Let us pass through your land to our country.’
text.Rotherham: Then did Israel send messengers unto Sihon king of the Amorites, king of Heshbon,—and Israel said unto him, Let us pass, we pray thee, through thy land as far as my own place.
text.KJV: And Israel sent messengers unto Sihon king of the Amorites, the king of Heshbon; and Israel said unto him, Let us pass, we pray thee, through thy land into my place.
text.NABRE: Then Israel sent messengers to the Amorite king Sihon, who was king of Heshbon. Israel said to him, “Let me pass through your land to my own place.”
text.DRB: Israel therefore sent messengers to Sehon the king of the Amorrheites, who dwelt in Hesebon, & they said to him: Suffer me to pass through thy land unto the river.

text.NRSV: But Sihon did not trust Israel to pass through his territory; so Sihon gathered all his people together, and encamped at Jahaz, and fought with Israel.
text.Rotherham: But Sihon trusted not Israel, to pass through his boundary, so Sihon gathered together all his people, and encamped in Jahaz,—and fought against Israel.
text.KJV: But Sihon trusted not Israel to pass through his coast: but Sihon gathered all his people together, and pitched in Jahaz, and fought against Israel.
text.NABRE: But Sihon refused to let Israel pass through his territory. He gathered all his soldiers, and they encamped at Jahaz and fought Israel.
text.DRB: Who also himself despising the words of Israel, suffered him not to pass through his borders: but gathering an infinite multitude went forth against him into Jasa, & resisted strongly.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord, the God of Israel, gave Sihon and all his people into the hand of Israel, and they defeated them; so Israel occupied all the land of the Amorites, who inhabited that country.
text.Rotherham: Then did Yahweh, God of Israel, deliver up Sihon, and all his people, into the hand of Israel, and they smote them,—so Israel took possession of all the land of the Amorites, the inhabitants of that land.
text.KJV: And the Lord God of Israel delivered Sihon and all his people into the hand of Israel, and they smote them: so Israel possessed all the land of the Amorites, the inhabitants of that country.
text.NABRE: But the Lord, the God of Israel, delivered Sihon and his entire army into the power of Israel, who defeated them and occupied all the land of the Amorites who lived in that region.
text.DRB: And our Lord delivered him into the hands of Israel with all his army, & he struck him, & possessed all the Land of the Amorrhite the inhabiter of that country,

text.NRSV: They occupied all the territory of the Amorites from the Arnon to the Jabbok and from the wilderness to the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: Yea they took possession of all the territory of the Amorites,—from Arnon, even as far as the Jabbok, and from the desert, even as far as the Jordan.
text.KJV: And they possessed all the coasts of the Amorites, from Arnon even unto Jabbok, and from the wilderness even unto Jordan.
text.NABRE: They occupied all of the Amorite territory from the Arnon to the Jabbok and the wilderness to the Jordan.
text.DRB: and all the coasts thereof from Arnon unto Iaboc, and from the wilderness unto Jordan.

text.NRSV: So now the Lord, the God of Israel, has conquered the Amorites for the benefit of his people Israel. Do you intend to take their place?
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, it was, Yahweh God of Israel, that dispossessed the Amorites from before his people Israel,—and shalt, thou, possess it?
text.KJV: So now the Lord God of Israel hath dispossessed the Amorites from before his people Israel, and shouldest thou possess it?
text.NABRE: Now, then, it was the Lord, the God of Israel, who dispossessed the Amorites for his people, Israel. And you are going to dispossess them?
text.DRB: Our Lord therefore the God of Israel subverted the Amorrhite, his people of Israel fighting against him, and wilt thou now possess his land?

text.NRSV: Should you not possess what your god Chemosh gives you to possess? And should we not be the ones to possess everything that the Lord our God has conquered for our benefit?
text.Rotherham: What Chemosh thy god giveth thee to possess, that, wilt thou not possess? and, whatsoever Yahweh our God hath set before us to possess, that, shall we not possess?
text.KJV: Wilt not thou possess that which Chemosh thy god giveth thee to possess? So whomsoever the Lord our God shall drive out from before us, them will we possess.
text.NABRE: Should you not take possession of that which your god Chemosh gave you to possess, and should we not take possession of all that the Lord, our God, has dispossessed for us?
text.DRB: Are not those things which Chamos thy god possessed, due to thee by right? But the things that our Lord God hath obtained conqueror, shall come to our possession:

text.NRSV: Now are you any better than King Balak son of Zippor of Moab? Did he ever enter into conflict with Israel, or did he ever go to war with them?
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, art thou, really better, than Balak son of Zippor, king of Moab? Hath there been any, striving at all, with Israel, or any, fighting at all, with them,
text.KJV: And now art thou any thing better than Balak the son of Zippor, king of Moab? did he ever strive against Israel, or did he ever fight against them,
text.NABRE: Now, then, are you any better than Balak, son of Zippor, king of Moab? Did he ever quarrel with Israel or make war against them?
text.DRB: unless perhaps thou be better than Balac the son of Sephor the king of Moab: or canst shew, that he wrangled against Israel, and fought against him,

text.NRSV: While Israel lived in Heshbon and its villages, and in Aroer and its villages, and in all the towns that are along the Arnon, three hundred years, why did you not recover them within that time?
text.Rotherham: all the time that Israel hath been dwelling in Heshbon and in her towns, and in Aroer and in her towns, and in all the cities that are on the banks of Arnon,—for three hundred years? Wherefore, then, have ye not made a rescue within that time?
text.KJV: While Israel dwelt in Heshbon and her towns, and in Aroer and her towns, and in all the cities that be along by the coasts of Arnon, three hundred years? why therefore did ye not recover them within that time?
text.NABRE: Israel has dwelt in Heshbon and its villages, Aroer and its villages, and all the cities on the banks of the Arnon for three hundred years. Why did you not recover them during that time?
text.DRB: when he dwelt in Hesebon, & the little towns thereof, & in Aroer, & the towns thereof, or in all the cities near Jordan, for three hundred years. Wherefore have you so long attempted nothing for reclaim?

text.NRSV: It is not I who have sinned against you, but you are the one who does me wrong by making war on me. Let the Lord, who is judge, render judgment this day between the Israelites and the Ammonites.”
text.Rotherham: I, therefore, have not sinned against thee; but, thou, art doing me a wrong, in fighting against me,—Let Yahweh, the Judge, give judgment today, between the sons of Israel, and the sons of Ammon!
text.KJV: Wherefore I have not sinned against thee, but thou doest me wrong to war against me: the Lord the Judge be judge this day between the children of Israel and the children of Ammon.
text.NABRE: As for me, I have not sinned against you, but you wrong me by making war against me. Let the Lord, who is judge, decide this day between the Israelites and the Ammonites!’”
text.DRB: Therefore I do not sin against thee, but thou doest evil against me, denouncing me unjust wars. Our Lord be judge the arbiter of this day between Israel, and between the children of Ammon.

text.NRSV: But the king of the Ammonites did not heed the message that Jephthah sent him.
text.Rotherham: But the king of the sons of Ammon hearkened not unto the words of Jephthah, which he sent unto him.
text.KJV: Howbeit the king of the children of Ammon hearkened not unto the words of Jephthah which he sent him.
text.NABRE: But the king of the Ammonites paid no heed to the message Jephthah sent him.
text.DRB: And the king of the children of Ammon would not harken to the words of Jephte, which he sent him by the messengers.

text.NRSV: Then the spirit of the Lord came upon Jephthah, and he passed through Gilead and Manasseh. He passed on to Mizpah of Gilead, and from Mizpah of Gilead he passed on to the Ammonites.
text.Rotherham: Then came upon Jephthah the spirit of Yahweh, and he passed through Gilead and Manasseh,—and passed through Mizpeh of Gilead, and, from Mizpeh of Gilead, he passed through [unto] the sons of Ammon.
text.KJV: Then the Spirit of the Lord came upon Jephthah, and he passed over Gilead, and Manasseh, and passed over Mizpeh of Gilead, and from Mizpeh of Gilead he passed over unto the children of Ammon.
text.NABRE: Jephthah’s Vow. The spirit of the Lord came upon Jephthah. He passed through Gilead and Manasseh, and through Mizpah of Gilead as well, and from Mizpah of Gilead he crossed over against the Ammonites.
text.DRB: Therefore the spirit of our Lord came upon Jephte, and circuiting Galaad, and Manasses, Maspha also of Galaad, and thence passing to the children of Ammon,

text.NRSV: And Jephthah made a vow to the Lord, and said, “If you will give the Ammonites into my hand,
text.Rotherham: And Jephthah vowed a vow unto Yahweh, and said,—If thou wilt, deliver, the sons of Ammon into my hand,
text.KJV: And Jephthah vowed a vow unto the Lord, and said, If thou shalt without fail deliver the children of Ammon into mine hands,
text.NABRE: Jephthah made a vow to the Lord. “If you deliver the Ammonites into my power,” he said,
text.DRB: he vowed a vow to our Lord, saying: If thou wilt deliver the children of Ammon into my hands,

text.NRSV: then whoever comes out of the doors of my house to meet me, when I return victorious from the Ammonites, shall be the Lord’s, to be offered up by me as a burnt offering.”
text.Rotherham: then shall it be, that, whosoever cometh forth out of the doors of my house, to meet me, when I return successful, from the sons of Ammon, shall belong unto Yahweh, and I will offer him up, as an ascending-sacrifice.
text.KJV: Then it shall be, that whatsoever cometh forth of the doors of my house to meet me, when I return in peace from the children of Ammon, shall surely be the Lord’s, and I will offer it up for a burnt offering.
text.NABRE: “whoever comes out of the doors of my house to meet me when I return from the Ammonites in peace shall belong to the Lord. I shall offer him up as a burnt offering.”
text.DRB: whosoever shalt first come forth out of the doors of my house, and shall meet me returning with peace from the children of Ammon, him will I offer an holocaust to our Lord.

text.NRSV: So Jephthah crossed over to the Ammonites to fight against them; and the Lord gave them into his hand.
text.Rotherham: So then Jephthah passed over unto the sons of Ammon, to fight against them,—and Yahweh delivered them into his hand;
text.KJV: So Jephthah passed over unto the children of Ammon to fight against them; and the Lord delivered them into his hands.
text.NABRE: Jephthah then crossed over against the Ammonites to fight against them, and the Lord delivered them into his power.
text.DRB: And Jephte passed to the children of Ammon, to fight against them: whom our Lord delivered into his hands.

text.NRSV: He inflicted a massive defeat on them from Aroer to the neighborhood of Minnith, twenty towns, and as far as Abel-keramim. So the Ammonites were subdued before the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: and he smote them, from Aroer even till thou enterest in to Minnith, even twenty cities, and as far as Abel-keramim, with an exceeding great smiting,—and thus were the sons of Ammon subdued before the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And he smote them from Aroer, even till thou come to Minnith, even twenty cities, and unto the plain of the vineyards, with a very great slaughter. Thus the children of Ammon were subdued before the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: He inflicted a very severe defeat on them from Aroer to the approach of Minnith—twenty cities in all—and as far as Abel-keramin. So the Ammonites were brought into subjection by the Israelites.
text.DRB: And he struck from Aroer till thou come to Mennith, twenty cities, and as far as Abel, which is set with vineyards, with a very great plague. And the children of Ammon were humbled by the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: Then Jephthah came to his home at Mizpah; and there was his daughter coming out to meet him with timbrels and with dancing. She was his only child; he had no son or daughter except her.
text.Rotherham: Then came Jephthah towards Mizpah, unto his own house, and lo! his daughter, coming forth to meet him, with timbrels, and with dances,—and, she, was none other than his only child, he had not, besides her, either son or daughter.
text.KJV: And Jephthah came to Mizpeh unto his house, and, behold, his daughter came out to meet him with timbrels and with dances: and she was his only child; beside her he had neither son nor daughter.
text.NABRE: When Jephthah returned to his house in Mizpah, it was his daughter who came out to meet him, with tambourine-playing and dancing. She was his only child: he had neither son nor daughter besides her.
text.DRB: But Jephte returning into Maspha to his house, his only begotten daughter met him with timbrels and dances. For he had not other children.

text.NRSV: When he saw her, he tore his clothes, and said, “Alas, my daughter! You have brought me very low; you have become the cause of great trouble to me. For I have opened my mouth to the Lord, and I cannot take back my vow.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when he saw her, that he rent his clothes, and said—Alas! my daughter, Thou hast, brought me low, Even, thou, hast come to be among them who trouble me,—Yet, I, opened wide my mouth unto Yahweh, and cannot go back.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when he saw her, that he rent his clothes, and said, Alas, my daughter! thou hast brought me very low, and thou art one of them that trouble me: for I have opened my mouth unto the Lord, and I cannot go back.
text.NABRE: When he saw her, he tore his garments and said, “Ah, my daughter! You have struck me down and brought calamity upon me. For I have made a vow to the Lord and I cannot take it back.”
text.DRB: Whom when he saw, he rent his garments, and said: Woe is me my daughter, thou hast deceived me, and thyself art deceived: for I have opened my mouth to our Lord, and I can do no other thing.

text.NRSV: She said to him, “My father, if you have opened your mouth to the Lord, do to me according to what has gone out of your mouth, now that the Lord has given you vengeance against your enemies, the Ammonites.”
text.Rotherham: And she said unto him—My father! Thou hast opened wide thy mouth unto Yahweh, Do with me, according to that which hath gone forth out of thy mouth,—after that Yahweh hath exacted for thee an avenging from thine enemies, from the sons of Ammon.
text.KJV: And she said unto him, My father, if thou hast opened thy mouth unto the Lord, do to me according to that which hath proceeded out of thy mouth; forasmuch as the Lord hath taken vengeance for thee of thine enemies, even of the children of Ammon.
text.NABRE: “Father,” she replied, “you have made a vow to the Lord. Do with me as you have vowed, because the Lord has taken vengeance for you against your enemies the Ammonites.”
text.DRB: To whom she answered: My father, if thou hast opened thy mouth to our Lord, do unto me whatsoever thou hast promised, the revenge & victory of thine enemies being granted to thee.

text.NRSV: And she said to her father, “Let this thing be done for me: Grant me two months, so that I may go and wander on the mountains, and bewail my virginity, my companions and I.”
text.Rotherham: And she said unto her father, Let this thing, be done for me,—Let me alone two months, that I may depart, and go down upon the mountains, and bewail my virginity, I and my companions.
text.KJV: And she said unto her father, Let this thing be done for me: let me alone two months, that I may go up and down upon the mountains, and bewail my virginity, I and my fellows.
text.NABRE: Then she said to her father, “Let me have this favor. Do nothing for two months, that I and my companions may go wander in the mountains to weep for my virginity.”
text.DRB: And she said to her father: This only grant me which I desire: Suffer me that two months I may go about the mountains, & bewail my virginity with my fellows.

text.NRSV: “Go,” he said and sent her away for two months. So she departed, she and her companions, and bewailed her virginity on the mountains.
text.Rotherham: And he said—Depart. So he let her go, for two months,—and she departed, she and her companions, and bewailed her virginity, upon the mountains.
text.KJV: And he said, Go. And he sent her away for two months: and she went with her companions, and bewailed her virginity upon the mountains.
text.NABRE: “Go,” he replied, and sent her away for two months. So she departed with her companions and wept for her virginity in the mountains.
text.DRB: To whom he answered: Go. And he dismissed her two months. And when she was gone with her fellows and companions, she mourned her virginity in the mountains.

text.NRSV: At the end of two months, she returned to her father, who did with her according to the vow he had made. She had never slept with a man. So there arose an Israelite custom that
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, at the end of two months, that she returned unto her father, and he fulfilled on her his vow which he had vowed,—she not having known man. And it became a statute, in Israel:—
text.KJV: And it came to pass at the end of two months, that she returned unto her father, who did with her according to his vow which he had vowed: and she knew no man. And it was a custom in Israel,
text.NABRE: At the end of the two months she returned to her father, and he did to her as he had vowed. She had not had relations with any man. It became a custom in Israel
text.DRB: And the two months being expired, she returned to her father, and he did to her as he had vowed, who knew not man. Thence-forth a fashion in Israel, and a custom was kept:

text.NRSV: for four days every year the daughters of Israel would go out to lament the daughter of Jephthah the Gileadite.
text.Rotherham: From year to year, departed the daughters of Israel, to lament aloud for the daughter of Jephthah, the Gileadite, four days in the year.
text.KJV: That the daughters of Israel went yearly to lament the daughter of Jephthah the Gileadite four days in a year.
text.NABRE: for Israelite women to go yearly to mourn the daughter of Jephthah the Gileadite for four days of the year.
text.DRB: that after the compass of a year the daughters of Israel assemble together, and mourn the daughter of Jephte the Galaadite four days.

text.NRSV: The men of Ephraim were called to arms, and they crossed to Zaphon and said to Jephthah, “Why did you cross over to fight against the Ammonites, and did not call us to go with you? We will burn your house down over you!”
text.Rotherham: And the men of Ephraim were called together, and passed over northward,—and said unto Jephthah—Wherefore didst thou pass over to do battle with the sons of Ammon, and, for us, didst not call, to go with thee? Thy house, will we consume over thee with fire.
text.KJV: And the men of Ephraim gathered themselves together, and went northward, and said unto Jephthah, Wherefore passedst thou over to fight against the children of Ammon, and didst not call us to go with thee? we will burn thine house upon thee with fire.
text.NABRE: The men of Ephraim were called out, and they crossed over to Zaphon. They said to Jephthah, “Why did you go to fight with the Ammonites without calling us to go with you? We will burn your house on top of you.”
text.DRB: But behold in Ephraim there arose a sedition. For they passing against the North, said to Jephte: Going to fight against the children of Ammon, why wouldst thou not call us, that we might go with thee? Therefore we will burn thy house.

text.NRSV: Jephthah said to them, “My people and I were engaged in conflict with the Ammonites who oppressed us severely. But when I called you, you did not deliver me from their hand.
text.Rotherham: And Jephthah said unto them, Great strife, had, I and my people, even with the sons of Ammon; but, when I cried unto you for help, ye saved me not out of their hand.
text.KJV: And Jephthah said unto them, I and my people were at great strife with the children of Ammon; and when I called you, ye delivered me not out of their hands.
text.NABRE: Jephthah answered them, “My soldiers and I were engaged in a contest with the Ammonites. They were pressing us hard, and I cried out to you, but you did not come to save me from their power.
text.DRB: To whom he answered: I and my people were at great strife against the children of Ammon: & I called you, that you should aid me, and you would not do it.

text.NRSV: When I saw that you would not deliver me, I took my life in my hand, and crossed over against the Ammonites, and the Lord gave them into my hand. Why then have you come up to me this day, to fight against me?”
text.Rotherham: So, when I saw that thou wast not going to save, then put I my life in my hand, and passed over against the sons of Ammon, and Yahweh delivered them into my hand. Wherefore, then, have ye come up against me this day, to fight against me?
text.KJV: And when I saw that ye delivered me not, I put my life in my hands, and passed over against the children of Ammon, and the Lord delivered them into my hand: wherefore then are ye come up unto me this day, to fight against me?
text.NABRE: When I saw that you were not coming to save me, I took my life in my own hand and crossed over against the Ammonites, and the Lord delivered them into my power. Why, then, should you come up against me this day to fight with me?”
text.DRB: Which I seeing put my life in mine own hands, and passed to the children of Ammon, and our Lord delivered them into my hands. What have I deserved, that you rise against me in battle.

text.NRSV: Then Jephthah gathered all the men of Gilead and fought with Ephraim; and the men of Gilead defeated Ephraim, because they said, “You are fugitives from Ephraim, you Gileadites—in the heart of Ephraim and Manasseh.”
text.Rotherham: And Jephthah gathered together all the men of Gilead, and fought with Ephraim,—and the men of Gilead smote Ephraim, because they said—Fugitives of Ephraim, are, ye, O ye Gileadites, in the midst of Ephraim, in the midst of Manasseh.
text.KJV: Then Jephthah gathered together all the men of Gilead, and fought with Ephraim: and the men of Gilead smote Ephraim, because they said, Ye Gileadites are fugitives of Ephraim among the Ephraimites, and among the Manassites.
text.NABRE: Then Jephthah gathered together all the men of Gilead and fought against Ephraim. The men of Gilead defeated Ephraim,
text.DRB: Therefore all the men of Galaad being called to him, he fought against Ephraim: and the men of Galaad struck Ephraim, because he had said: Galaad is a fugitive of Ephraim, & dwelleth in the midst of Ephraim & Manasses.

text.NRSV: Then the Gileadites took the fords of the Jordan against the Ephraimites. Whenever one of the fugitives of Ephraim said, “Let me go over,” the men of Gilead would say to him, “Are you an Ephraimite?” When he said, “No,”
text.Rotherham: And the Gileadites captured the fords of the Jordan, against the Ephraimites,—and so it was, that, when the fugitives of Ephraim said, Let me pass over, the men of Gilead said to him, An Ephraimite, art thou? and, if he said—Nay!
text.KJV: And the Gileadites took the passages of Jordan before the Ephraimites: and it was so, that when those Ephraimites which were escaped said, Let me go over; that the men of Gilead said unto him, Art thou an Ephraimite? If he said, Nay;
text.NABRE: and Gilead seized the fords of the Jordan against Ephraim. When any of the fleeing Ephraimites said, “Let me pass,” the men of Gilead would say to him, “Are you an Ephraimite?” If he answered, “No!”
text.DRB: And the Galaadites took the fords of Jordan, by the which Ephraim was to return. And when there had come to the same one of the number of Ephraim, flying, and had said: I beseech you let me pass: The Galaadites said to him: Art thou not an Ephraite? Who saying: I am not:

text.NRSV: they said to him, “Then say Shibboleth,” and he said, “Sibboleth,” for he could not pronounce it right. Then they seized him and killed him at the fords of the Jordan. Forty-two thousand of the Ephraimites fell at that time.
text.Rotherham: they said to him, Come now, say Shibboleth, and, if he said Sibboleth, and he could not take heed to speak in that manner, then laid they hold on him, and slew him at the fords of the Jordan,—and there fell, at that time, of the Ephraimites, forty-two thousand.
text.KJV: Then said they unto him, Say now Shibboleth: and he said Sibboleth: for he could not frame to pronounce it right. Then they took him, and slew him at the passages of Jordan: and there fell at that time of the Ephraimites forty and two thousand.
text.NABRE: they would ask him to say “Shibboleth.” If he said “Sibboleth,” not pronouncing it exactly right, they would seize him and kill him at the fords of the Jordan. Forty-two thousand Ephraimites fell at that time.
text.DRB: they asked him: Say then Schibboleth, which is interpreted an ear of corn. Who answered, Sibboleth, not being able by the same letter to express, an ear of corn. And immediately being apprehended they killed him in the very passage of Jordan. And there fell at that time of Ephraim two and forty thousand.

text.NRSV: Jephthah judged Israel six years. Then Jephthah the Gileadite died, and was buried in his town in Gilead.
text.Rotherham: And, when Jephthah had judged Israel six years, then Jephthah the Gileadite died, and was buried in [one of] the cities of Gilead.
text.KJV: And Jephthah judged Israel six years. Then died Jephthah the Gileadite, and was buried in one of the cities of Gilead.
text.NABRE: Jephthah judged Israel for six years, and Jephthah the Gileadite died and was buried in his city in Gilead.
text.DRB: Therefore Jephte the Galaadite judged Israel six years: and he died, and was buried in his city of Galaad.

text.NRSV: After him Ibzan of Bethlehem judged Israel.
text.Rotherham: And there judged Israel, after him, Ibzan of Bethlehem.
text.KJV: And after him Ibzan of Beth–lehem judged Israel.
text.NABRE: Ibzan. After him Ibzan of Bethlehem judged Israel.
text.DRB: After him Abesan of Bethlehem judged Israel:

text.NRSV: He had thirty sons. He gave his thirty daughters in marriage outside his clan and brought in thirty young women from outside for his sons. He judged Israel seven years.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, he had thirty sons, and, thirty daughters, sent he abroad, while, thirty daughters, brought he from abroad for his sons. And he judged Israel seven years.
text.KJV: And he had thirty sons, and thirty daughters, whom he sent abroad, and took in thirty daughters from abroad for his sons. And he judged Israel seven years.
text.NABRE: He had thirty sons and thirty daughters whom he gave in marriage outside the family, while bringing in thirty wives for his sons from outside the family. He judged Israel for seven years.
text.DRB: who had thirty sons, and as many daughters, which he sending abroad, gave to husbands, and took wives for his sons of the same number, bringing them into his house. Who judged Israel seven years:

text.NRSV: Then Ibzan died, and was buried at Bethlehem.
text.Rotherham: And Ibzan died, and was buried in Bethlehem.
text.KJV: Then died Ibzan, and was buried at Beth–lehem.
text.NABRE: Ibzan died and was buried in Bethlehem.
text.DRB: and died and was buried in Bethlehem.

text.NRSV: After him Elon the Zebulunite judged Israel; and he judged Israel ten years.
text.Rotherham: And, after him, did Elon the Zebulunite judge Israel,—and he judged Israel, ten years.
text.KJV: And after him Elon, a Zebulonite, judged Israel; and he judged Israel ten years.
text.NABRE: Elon. After him Elon the Zebulunite judged Israel; he judged Israel for ten years.
text.DRB: To whom succeeded Ahialon a Zabulonite: and he judged Israel ten years:

text.NRSV: Then Elon the Zebulunite died, and was buried at Aijalon in the land of Zebulun.
text.Rotherham: And Elon the Zebulunite died, and was buried in Aijalon, in the land of Zebulun.
text.KJV: And Elon the Zebulonite died, and was buried in Aijalon in the country of Zebulun.
text.NABRE: Elon the Zebulunite died and was buried at Aijalon in the land of Zebulun.
text.DRB: and he died and was buried in Zabulon.

text.NRSV: After him Abdon son of Hillel the Pirathonite judged Israel.
text.Rotherham: And there judged Israel, after him, Abdon son of Hillel, the Pirathonite.
text.KJV: And after him Abdon the son of Hillel, a Pirathonite, judged Israel.
text.NABRE: Abdon. After him Abdon, son of Hillel, the Pirathonite, judged Israel.
text.DRB: After him Abdon judged Israel, the son of Illel a Pharathonite:

text.NRSV: He had forty sons and thirty grandsons, who rode on seventy donkeys; he judged Israel eight years.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, he had forty sons and thirty grandsons, who rode on seventy ass colts,—and he judged Israel eight years.
text.KJV: And he had forty sons and thirty nephews, that rode on threescore and ten ass colts: and he judged Israel eight years.
text.NABRE: He had forty sons and thirty grandsons, who rode on seventy donkeys. He judged Israel for eight years.
text.DRB: who had forty sons, and of them thirty nephews, mounting upon seventy ass colts, and he judged Israel eight years:

text.NRSV: Then Abdon son of Hillel the Pirathonite died, and was buried at Pirathon in the land of Ephraim, in the hill country of the Amalekites.
text.Rotherham: And Abdon son of Hillel, the Pirathonite died,—and was buried in Pirathon, in the land of Ephraim, in the hill country of the Amalekites.
text.KJV: And Abdon the son of Hillel the Pirathonite died, and was buried in Pirathon in the land of Ephraim, in the mount of the Amalekites.
text.NABRE: Abdon, son of Hillel, the Pirathonite, died and was buried in Pirathon in the land of Ephraim in the mountain region of the Amalekites.
text.DRB: and he died, and was buried in Pharathon of the Land of Ephraim, in the mount of Amalec.

text.NRSV: The Israelites again did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, and the Lord gave them into the hand of the Philistines forty years.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel again did the thing that was wicked in the sight of Yahweh,—so Yahweh delivered them up into the hand of the Philistines, forty years.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel did evil again in the sight of the Lord; and the Lord delivered them into the hand of the Philistines forty years.
text.NABRE: The Israelites again did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, who therefore delivered them into the power of the Philistines for forty years.
text.DRB: And again the children of Israel did evil in the sight of our Lord: who delivered them into the hands of the Philistiims forty years.

text.NRSV: There was a certain man of Zorah, of the tribe of the Danites, whose name was Manoah. His wife was barren, having borne no children.
text.Rotherham: And there was a certain man of Zorah, of the family of the Danites, and, his name, was Manoah; and, his wife, was barren, and had borne no child.
text.KJV: And there was a certain man of Zorah, of the family of the Danites, whose name was Manoah; and his wife was barren, and bare not.
text.NABRE: There was a certain man from Zorah, of the clan of the Danites, whose name was Manoah. His wife was barren and had borne no children.
text.DRB: And there was a certain man of Saraa, and of the stock of Dan, named Manue, having a wife barren.

text.NRSV: And the angel of the Lord appeared to the woman and said to her, “Although you are barren, having borne no children, you shall conceive and bear a son.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh appeared unto the woman,—and said unto her—Lo! I pray thee, thou, art barren, and hast borne no child, but thou shalt conceive, and shalt bear a son.
text.KJV: And the angel of the Lord appeared unto the woman, and said unto her, Behold now, thou art barren, and bearest not: but thou shalt conceive, and bear a son.
text.NABRE: An angel of the Lord appeared to the woman and said to her: Though you are barren and have had no children, you will conceive and bear a son.
text.DRB: To whom an Angel of our Lord appeared, and said to her: Thou art barren and without children: but thou shalt conceive & bear a son:

text.NRSV: Now be careful not to drink wine or strong drink, or to eat anything unclean,
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, beware, I pray thee, and do not drink wine or strong drink,—nor eat anything unclean;
text.KJV: Now therefore beware, I pray thee, and drink not wine nor strong drink, and eat not any unclean thing:
text.NABRE: Now, then, be careful to drink no wine or beer and to eat nothing unclean,
text.DRB: beware therefore that thou drink not wine & sicer, nor eat any unclean thing:

text.NRSV: for you shall conceive and bear a son. No razor is to come on his head, for the boy shall be a nazirite to God from birth. It is he who shall begin to deliver Israel from the hand of the Philistines.”
text.Rotherham: for lo! thou, art about to conceive and bear a son, and no, razor, shall come on his head, for, one separate unto God, shall the boy be from his birth,—and, he, shall begin to save Israel out of the hand of the Philistines.
text.KJV: For, lo, thou shalt conceive, and bear a son; and no razor shall come on his head: for the child shall be a Nazarite unto God from the womb: and he shall begin to deliver Israel out of the hand of the Philistines.
text.NABRE: for you will conceive and bear a son. No razor shall touch his head, for the boy is to be a nazirite for God from the womb. It is he who will begin to save Israel from the power of the Philistines.
text.DRB: because thou shalt conceive & bear a son, whose head the razor shall not touch: for he shall be a Nazarite of God, from his infancy, and from his mother's womb, and he shall begin to deliver Israel from the hands of the Philistiims.

text.NRSV: Then the woman came and told her husband, “A man of God came to me, and his appearance was like that of an angel of God, most awesome; I did not ask him where he came from, and he did not tell me his name;
text.Rotherham: So the woman came in, and told her husband, saying, A man of God, came unto me, and, his, appearance, was as the appearance of the messenger of God, reverend exceedingly,—and I asked him not whence he was, and, his name, he told me not.
text.KJV: Then the woman came and told her husband, saying, A man of God came unto me, and his countenance was like the countenance of an angel of God, very terrible: but I asked him not whence he was, neither told he me his name:
text.NABRE: The woman went and told her husband, “A man of God came to me; he had the appearance of an angel of God, fearsome indeed. I did not ask him where he came from, nor did he tell me his name.
text.DRB: Who when she was come to her husband, said to him: A man of God came to me, having an Angelical countenance, exceeding terrible. Whom when I had asked, who he was, and whence he came, and by what name he was called, he would not tell me:

text.NRSV: but he said to me, ‘You shall conceive and bear a son. So then drink no wine or strong drink, and eat nothing unclean, for the boy shall be a nazirite to God from birth to the day of his death.’”
text.Rotherham: But he said unto me, Lo! thou art about to conceive, and bear a son,—now, therefore, do not drink wine or strong drink, nor eat anything unclean, for, one separate unto God, shall the boy be, from his birth until the day of his death.
text.KJV: But he said unto me, Behold, thou shalt conceive, and bear a son; and now drink no wine nor strong drink, neither eat any unclean thing: for the child shall be a Nazarite to God from the womb to the day of his death.
text.NABRE: But he said to me, ‘You will conceive and bear a son. So drink no wine or beer, and eat nothing unclean. For the boy shall be a nazirite for God from the womb, until the day of his death.’”
text.DRB: but this he answered: Behold thou shalt conceive and bear a son: beware thou drink not wine nor sicer, and that thou eat not any unclean thing: for the child shall be the Nazarite of God from his infancy, and from his mother's womb until the day of his death.

text.NRSV: Then Manoah entreated the Lord, and said, “O, Lord, I pray, let the man of God whom you sent come to us again and teach us what we are to do concerning the boy who will be born.”
text.Rotherham: Then Manoah made entreaty unto Yahweh, and said: Pardon, O My Lord! the man of God whom thou didst send, I pray thee, let him come again unto us, that he may teach us what we are to do, unto the boy that is to be born.
text.KJV: Then Manoah intreated the Lord, and said, O my Lord, let the man of God which thou didst send come again unto us, and teach us what we shall do unto the child that shall be born.
text.NABRE: Manoah then prayed to the Lord. “Please, my Lord,” he said, “may the man of God whom you sent return to us to teach us what to do for the boy who is to be born.”
text.DRB: Manue therefore prayed to our Lord, and said: I beseech thee, o Lord, that the man of God, whom thou didst send, may come again, and teach us what we ought to do concerning the child that shall be born.

text.NRSV: God listened to Manoah, and the angel of God came again to the woman as she sat in the field; but her husband Manoah was not with her.
text.Rotherham: And God hearkened unto the voice of Manoah,—and the messenger of God came again unto the woman, as, she, was sitting in the field, Manoah her husband, not being with her.
text.KJV: And God hearkened to the voice of Manoah; and the angel of God came again unto the woman as she sat in the field: but Manoah her husband was not with her.
text.NABRE: God heard the prayer of Manoah, and the angel of God came again to the woman as she was sitting in the field; but her husband Manoah was not with her.
text.DRB: And our Lord heard Manue praying, and the Angel of our Lord appeared again to his wife sitting in the field, but Manue her husband was not with her. Who when she had seen the Angel,

text.NRSV: So the woman ran quickly and told her husband, “The man who came to me the other day has appeared to me.”
text.Rotherham: So the woman made haste, and ran, and told her husband,—and said unto him, Lo! the man, hath appeared unto me, who came the other day unto me.
text.KJV: And the woman made haste, and ran, and shewed her husband, and said unto him, Behold, the man hath appeared unto me, that came unto me the other day.
text.NABRE: The woman ran quickly and told her husband. “The man who came to me the other day has appeared to me,” she said to him;
text.DRB: hastened, and ran to her husband: and she told him, saying: Behold the man hath appeared to me, whom I saw before.

text.NRSV: Manoah got up and followed his wife, and came to the man and said to him, “Are you the man who spoke to this woman?” And he said, “I am.”
text.Rotherham: Then Manoah arose, and followed his wife,—and came unto the man, and said unto him—Art, thou, the man that spake unto the wife? And he said—I am.
text.KJV: And Manoah arose, and went after his wife, and came to the man, and said unto him, Art thou the man that spakest unto the woman? And he said, I am.
text.NABRE: so Manoah got up and followed his wife. When he reached the man, he said to him, “Are you the one who spoke to my wife?” I am, he answered.
text.DRB: Who rose, and followed his wife: and coming to the man, said to him: Art thou he that didst speak to the woman? And he answered: I am.

text.NRSV: Then Manoah said, “Now when your words come true, what is to be the boy’s rule of life; what is he to do?”
text.Rotherham: And Manoah said, Now, let thy words come to pass,—What shall be the rule for the boy, and his work?
text.KJV: And Manoah said, Now let thy words come to pass. How shall we order the child, and how shall we do unto him?
text.NABRE: Then Manoah asked, “Now, when what you say comes true, what rules must the boy follow? What must he do?”
text.DRB: To whom Manue, when, said he, thy word shall be fulfilled, what wilt thou that the child do? Or from what shall he keep himself?

text.NRSV: The angel of the Lord said to Manoah, “Let the woman give heed to all that I said to her.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh said unto Manoah,—Of all that I said unto the woman, let her beware.
text.KJV: And the angel of the Lord said unto Manoah, Of all that I said unto the woman let her beware.
text.NABRE: The angel of the Lord answered Manoah: Your wife must be careful about all the things of which I spoke to her.
text.DRB: And the Angel of our Lord said to Manue: From all things, which I have spoken to thy wife, let him refrain himself:

text.NRSV: She may not eat of anything that comes from the vine. She is not to drink wine or strong drink, or eat any unclean thing. She is to observe everything that I commanded her.”
text.Rotherham: Of nothing that cometh of the vine, may she eat, nor wine nor strong drink, let her drink, nor, anything unclean, let her eat,—all that I commanded her, let her observe.
text.KJV: She may not eat of any thing that cometh of the vine, neither let her drink wine or strong drink, nor eat any unclean thing: all that I commanded her let her observe.
text.NABRE: She must not eat anything that comes from the vine, she must not drink wine or beer, and she must not eat anything unclean. Let her observe all that I have commanded her.
text.DRB: and whatsoever groweth of the vineyard, let him not eat: wine and sicer let him not drink, let him not eat any unclean thing: and whatsoever I have commanded her, let him fulfill and keep.

text.NRSV: Manoah said to the angel of the Lord, “Allow us to detain you, and prepare a kid for you.”
text.Rotherham: And Manoah said unto the messenger of Yahweh,—I pray thee, let us detain thee, that we may make ready for thy presence, a kid.
text.KJV: And Manoah said unto the angel of the Lord, I pray thee, let us detain thee, until we shall have made ready a kid for thee.
text.NABRE: Then Manoah said to the angel of the Lord, “Permit us to detain you, so that we may prepare a young goat for you.”
text.DRB: And Manue said to the Angel of our Lord: I beseech thee that thou condescend to my petitions, and let us make to thee a kid of goats.

text.NRSV: The angel of the Lord said to Manoah, “If you detain me, I will not eat your food; but if you want to prepare a burnt offering, then offer it to the Lord.” (For Manoah did not know that he was the angel of the Lord.)
text.Rotherham: But the messenger of Yahweh said unto Manoah—Though thou detain me, yet will I not eat of thy food, and, though thou make ready an ascending-sacrifice, unto Yahweh, must thou cause it to ascend. For Manoah had not discerned that, the angel of Yahweh, he was.
text.KJV: And the angel of the Lord said unto Manoah, Though thou detain me, I will not eat of thy bread: and if thou wilt offer a burnt offering, thou must offer it unto the Lord. For Manoah knew not that he was an angel of the Lord.
text.NABRE: But the angel of the Lord answered Manoah: Though you detained me, I would not eat your food. But if you want to prepare a burnt offering, then offer it up to the Lord. For Manoah did not know that he was the angel of the Lord.
text.DRB: to whom the Angel answered: If thou constrain me, I will not eat thy bread: but if thou wilt make holocaust, offer it to our Lord. And Manue knew not that it was an Angel of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Manoah said to the angel of the Lord, “What is your name, so that we may honor you when your words come true?”
text.Rotherham: Then said Manoah unto the messenger of Yahweh, What is thy name,—that, when thy word cometh to pass, we may do thee honour?
text.KJV: And Manoah said unto the angel of the Lord, What is thy name, that when thy sayings come to pass we may do thee honour?
text.NABRE: Then Manoah said to the angel of the Lord, “What is your name, that we may honor you when your words come true?”
text.DRB: And he said to him: What is thy name, that, if thy word shall be fulfilled, we may honour thee?

text.NRSV: But the angel of the Lord said to him, “Why do you ask my name? It is too wonderful.”
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh said unto him, Wherefore is it, that thou shouldst ask after my name,—seeing that, it, is Wonderful?
text.KJV: And the angel of the Lord said unto him, Why askest thou thus after my name, seeing it is secret?
text.NABRE: The angel of the Lord answered him: Why do you ask my name? It is wondrous.
text.DRB: To whom he answered: Why asketh thou my name, which is marvelous?

text.NRSV: So Manoah took the kid with the grain offering, and offered it on the rock to the Lord, to him who works wonders.
text.Rotherham: So Manoah took the kid, and the meal-offering, and caused them to ascend upon the rock unto Yahweh, who was about to do, wondrously, while Manoah and his wife were looking on.
text.KJV: So Manoah took a kid with a meat offering, and offered it upon a rock unto the Lord: and the angel did wondrously; and Manoah and his wife looked on.
text.NABRE: Then Manoah took a young goat with a grain offering and offered it on the rock to the Lord, who works wonders. While Manoah and his wife were looking on,
text.DRB: Manue therefore took a kid of the goats: and the libaments, & put them upon a rock, offering to our Lord, who doth marvelous things: & he and his wife looked on.
